title: About IIIT-NR
content:
Highlights 
 
   
 
 
 
 Established as a University by the Govt. Of Chhattisgarh 
 
 Recognised by UGC 
 
 Letter Approval 2020-21 as Accorded by AICTE 
 
 Letter Approval 2021-22 as Accorded by AICTE 
 
 Letter Approval 2022-23 as Accorded by AICTE 
 
 Letter Approval 2023-24 as Accorded by AICTE 
 
 Letter Approval 2024-25 as Accorded by AICTE 
 
 Letter Approval 2025-26 as Accorded by AICTE 
 
 Guided by a highly eminent board 
 
 Located in the capital of Chhattisgarh with good connectivity 
 
 Fully residential campus with state-of-the art infrastructure  
 
 Industry driven curriculum with focus on fostering innovation and entrepreneurship among students 
 
 Focus on teaching, research and consultancy  
 
 Equipped with e-enabled teaching and learning facilities 
 
 Intensive Professional English Language Development Facility                               
 
 
 Dr. SPM IIIT-Naya Raipur, established by the International Institute of Information Technology University Act, 2013 of the Government of Chhattisgarh, is a joint venture by Chhattisgarh State Government and National Thermal Power Corporation (NTPC). The institute is committed to pursue excellence in higher education, research and development in Information Technology (IT) and associated disciplines. It firmly believes in bestowing knowledge dissemination and imparting entrepreneurial skills to the students to enable them to address real world problems. To empower this vision, IIIT-NR fosters state-of-the-art research and product development laboratories to carry out inter-disciplinary research and product development endeavours.The institute is mentored by a highly eminent board comprising of :-  
 
   
 
 Prof. Dhananjai Pandey, Hon. Distinguished Professor, BHU (for life) and Distinguished Professor, IIT Bhilai 
 Chairman 
 
 Prof. U B Desai , The Founding Director of IIT Hyderabad 
 Member 
 
 Mr. Sujit Baksi, President Corporate Affairs,Tech Mahindra 
 Member 
 
 A. Pinto, Retired Lead Director of the Chairman's Supervisory Board of the Emirates Investment Bank, UAE 
 Member 
 
 Principal Secretary, Technical Education Department 
 Member 
 
 Principal Secretary, Electronics & Information Technology Department, Government of Chhattisgarh 
 Member 
 
 Director, Directorate of Technical Education Chhattisgarh 
 Member 
 
 Regional Executive Director (Western Region-II) NTPC Limited 
 Member 
 
 Chief General Manager, NTPC’s Lara Super Thermal Power Project 
 Member 
 
 Head  Power Management Institute, NTPC 
 Member 
 
 Vice Chancellor & Director, Dr. SPM IIIT-NR 
 Member Secretary 
 
 Dr. SPM IIIT-NR's fifty-acres residential campus is located in the newly developed smart city of Atal Nagar, approximately 23 kms from the city of Raipur. The vibrant lush green campus of IIIT-NR is enabled with Wi-Fi connectivity, CCTV surveillance, hi tech security measures and excellent amenities for sports and other recreational activities. The modern architecture of the buildings and the serene environment of the campus fosters an ambience for the students for creativity and innovation. The classrooms are equipped with state-of-the art technologies for teaching and learning.

title: Vision & Mission
content:
VISION 
 
 
 
 To be a leading technology institute that produces high quality IT professionals and fosters technological innovation for social upliftment. 
 
 MISSION 
 
 To create an Institute of Excellence in IT Education and Research for imparting knowledge through education, advancing knowledge through research, and providing service to the region, nation and mankind to contribute towards the creation of a better society.

title: Significance and Usage of Institute Logo
content:
The Institute has finalized and adopted its logo. Consistent and appropriate use of logo helps the communities easily connect to our Institute. While using IIIT-NR’s logo showcasing the content related to the Institute, please be aware of the description & usage guidelines: 
 
 Description of logo 
 
 The logo of DSPM IIIT-Naya Raipur has been designed to capture the institute’s vision towards its social responsibility along with its name and location. Its various components signify the following: 
 
 Buddha:  “Buddha” signifies enlightenment (an ideal state of intellectual and ethical perfection) through gain of knowledge, discipline, dedication and concentration. This object in the logo indicates that DSPM-IIIT Naya Raipur nurtures such qualities in its students and other members. 
 
 Circle Around Buddha : This artistically designed circle has both the letters ‘ C ’ and ‘ G ’ embedded, signifying Chhattisgarh. This object in the logo indicates that DSPM-IIIT Naya Raipur is located in and is supported by the State of Chhattisgarh. 
 
 Sanskrit Shloka : Taken from Bhagavad Gita (4:39), it means that ultimate wisdom and peace in life can be achieved only by acquiring knowledge. This object in the logo indicates that DSPM-IIIT Naya Raipur strives hard to impart as much knowledge as possible to its students and other members to help them lead a peaceful and contended life. 
 
   Institute Name in Long and  Short Forms: The logo also contains the institute’s name in both long and short forms. 
 
 In Nut Shell: The logo indicates that our institute is located in the State of Chhattisgarh and provides an environment to foster knowledge enrichment and development of humane qualities to enable individuals to emerge as enlightened citizens, who can lead a peaceful and contended life. 
 
 Usage Guidelines 
 
 When using the logo, please be careful of the following dont’s: 
 
 
 Don’t modify the logo’s placement & orientation of the symbol. 
 
 Don’t bevel or emboss the logo. 
 
 Don’t change the logo colours. 
 
 Don’t crop the logo in any way. 
 
 Don’t write anything on the logo. 
 
 Don’t place/display any other object on the logo. 
 
 Don’t use any such logo which is confusingly similar or dilutive of our logo. 
 
 
 When using the logo, please observe the following do’s: 
 
 
 Place the logo invariably at the top left hand corner with enough space around without any cluttering 
 
 While using in combination with other logos implying partnership, sponsorship or endorsement, the position and proportion must be appropriate. 
 
 Maintain the shape and proportion of logo and reproduce it at a legible size commensurate with the overall dimension of the letter pad, identity card, presentation, post, banner or flex.

title: Director
content:
Prof. Om Prakash Vyas Director, IIIT Naya Raipur 
 
 
 
 Prof. O.P. Vyas has recently been appointed as the Director of Dr. Shyama Prasad Mukherjee International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Naya Raipur. He is a senior academician, researcher, and institutional leader with over three decades of rich experience in higher technical education, research, and academic administration. 
 
 Prof. Vyas holds an M.Tech. in Computer Science from IIT Kharagpur, and pursued his Ph.D. research at TU Kaiserslautern, Germany, under the prestigious DAAD Fellowship program. He has contributed significantly during his tenure as Dean of Technology Development, Professor of Information Technology at IIIT Allahabad, where he played a key role in academic innovation, including the conceptualization and launch of the B.Tech. (IT–Business Informatics) program. 
 
 His areas of specialization include Data Science, Process Intelligence, Business Informatics, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) and Cyber Security. He has successfully led several national and international collaborative research projects, and served as Visiting Professor in countries such as France, Germany, Italy, Norway, and Japan. 
 
 Known for his academic vision, dedication to student-centric learning, and strategic leadership, Prof. Vyas aims to elevate IIIT Naya Raipur as a premier hub of technology, innovation, and multidisciplinary education aligned with the goals of India’s National Education Policy (NEP) 2020., while also contributing meaningfully to the state's technological growth. 
 
 A native of Raipur, Chhattisgarh, Prof. Vyas is widely regarded as a pioneer of IT education in the region. As the Founding Head of the Department of Computer Science at Pt. Ravishankar Shukla University, Raipur, he introduced the region’s first formal academic program in Computer Science in 1992. This landmark initiative laid the groundwork for the expansion of IT education across Chhattisgarh. Committed to inclusive growth, Prof. Vyas worked tirelessly to extend the reach of IT education to rural and remote areas of the state, bridging the digital divide and empowering underserved communities

title: Srinivasa K G
content:
Designation: (Professor (DSAI) and Dean(Academics)) 
 
 Qualification: Ph.D (Bangalore University), CMI Level 5 Awardee in Management and Leadership, BOYSCAST Fellow (DST), UGC Research Awardee 
 
 Department: DSAI 
 
 Email Id: srinivasa[at]iiitnr.edu.in 
 
 Contact No: 91-771-2474063 
 
 
 
 
 Brief Profile : 
 
 Dr. Srinivasa K G is a Professor of Data Science and Artificial Intelligence Programme at DSPM IIIT-Naya Raipur, C. G. India. Earlier he worked as a Professor at Information Management and Emerging Engineering Department of National Institute of Technical Teachers Training and Research, Chandigarh an autonomous Institute under Ministry of Education, Government of India. He also worked as an Associate Professor at CBP Government Engineering College, New Delhi (through UPSC) between 2016 – 19. He also served as Professor in the Department of CSE at M S Ramaiah Institute of Technology, Bangalore between 2003 – 2016. 
He received his Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangalore University in 2007. He is the recipient of All India Council for Technical Education – Career Award for Young Teachers, Indian Society of Technical Education – ISGITS National Award for Best Research Work Done by Young Teachers, Institution of Engineers (India) – IEI Young Engineer Award in Computer Engineering, Rajarambapu Patil National Award for Promising Engineering Teacher Award from ISTE – 2012, IMS Singapore – Visiting Scientist Fellowship Award. He has published more than 150 research papers in International Conferences and Journals. He has visited many Universities abroad as a visiting researcher – He has visited University of Oklahoma, USA, Iowa State University, USA, Hong Kong University, Korean University, National University of Singapore, University of British Columbia, Canada are his few prominent visits. He has authored many books in the area of Learning Analytics, Network Data Analytics, Soft Computing, Social Network Analysis, High Performance Computing, R Programming etc. with prestigious international publishers like Springer, TMH, Oxford, Cengage, and IGI Global. He has edited research monographs in the area of Cyber Physical Systems, Fog Computing and Energy Aware Computing with CRC Press and IGI Global. 
He has been awarded BOYSCAST Fellowship by DST, Govt. of India, for conducting post-doctoral research work at University of Melbourne, Australia. He is the principal Investigator for many funded projects from AICTE, UGC, DRDO, and DST. He has undertaken consultancy projects worth 60 lakhs towards conducting Professional Development Programmes under World Bank Project. He is the senior member of IEEE and ACM. His recent research areas include Innovative Teaching Practices in Engineering Education, pedagogy; outcomes based education, and teaching philosophy. 
 
 
 
 
 
 Research Interest 
 
 
 Data Mining, Cloud Computing, Internet of Things, Data Analytics, Cyber Physical Systems, Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality, Learning Analytics, Digital Pedagogy 
 
 
 
 
 Courses Offered 
 
 
 
 
 Introduction to Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, Autumn 2022, 2023, 2024 
 
 Discrete Mathematics, 2023 
 
 Introduction to Computer Programming, Spring 2023 
 
 Software Development, Spring 2024 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Responsibilities 
 
 
 
 
 Head of the Department (DSAI) : 2022 - 2024 
 
 Dean (Research and Innovation): 2023 - 2024 
 
 Registrar (Incharge): Feb 2024 - July 2024 
 
 Faculty Incharge (Incubation Centre) : 2023 - Till Date 
 
 Chief Finance and Accounts Officer (Incharge): June-July 2022 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Professional Activities 
 
 
 
 
 Professor (Information Management and Emerging Engineering), National Institute of Technical Teachers Training and Resarch, Chandigarh (March 2019 - March 2022) 
 
 Associate Professor (IT), CBP Government Engineering College, New Delhi (Dec. 2016 - March 2019) 
 
 Professor (CSE), MS Ramaiah Institute of Technology, Bangalore (March 2011 - Dec. 2016) 
 
 Visiting Academic (Department of Computer Science and Software Engineering), University of Melbourne, Australia (Aug 2010 - Feb. 2011) 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Awards And Accolades 
 
 
 
 
 Selected for UGC Research Award, 2016 -18 (RA-2016-18-GE-KAR-6789) 
 
 Recipient of BOYSCAST Fellowship from DST, 2009 - 2010 
 
 Recipient of ISTE Rajarambapu Patil National Award for Promising Engieering Teacher for Creative Works done in Technical Education, 2012 
 
 Recipient of IEI Young Engineers Award, 2008 
 
 Recipient of ISTE SGSITS National Award for Best Research Work done by Young Teachers, 2007 
 
 Recipient of AICTE Career Award for Young Teachers, 2005-2006. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Projects 
 
 
 
 
 Cyber Commando Training Programme Sponsored by MHA (Rs. 1.5 Crores) 
 
 AI Translation System from Gondi to Hindi and Vice Versa Sponsored by MoTA (Rs. 40 Lacs) 
 
 Study and Development of Deeplearning Modules for Radar Applications Sponsored by LRDE/DRDO (Rs. 9.79 Lacs) - NITTTR, Chandigarh 
 
 Design and Development of Assistive Internet of Things (IoT) Framework for Physically Challenged People under SERC Fast Track Proposal for Young Scientist Scheme of DST (Rs. 24 Lacs) - MSRIT, Bangalore 
 
 Emperical Analysis of Public Data Sources to Understand the Development Methodologies of Free and Open Source Software Engineering Sponsored by UGC (Rs. 10.35 Lacs) - MSRIT, Bangalore 
 
 Machine Learning Techniques for Data Mining based Intrusion Detection Systems Sponsored by DRDO (Rs. 14.95 Lacs) - MSRIT, Bangalore 
 
 Biomedical Data Mining and DNA Analysis Sponsored by AICTE (Rs. 10.5 Lacs) - MSRIT, Bangalore 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Research Supervision 
 
 
 
 
 Currently two students are working for PhD in the area of ARVR and Post Quantum Cryptography 
 
 Supervised 10 PhD Candidates in the areas of Grid Computing, Data Mining, Natural Language Processing, Free and Open Source Software Engineering, Cloud Computing, Machine Learning, Cognitive Applications to Radar Systems, and Parallel Algorithms.

title: Mithilesh Kumar Chaube
content:
Designation: (ASSOCIATE PROFESSOR ,Maths) 
 
 Qualification: Ph. D (IIT-BHU, Varanasi) 
 
 Department: SHM 
 
 Email Id: mithilesh[at]iiitnr.edu.in

 Brief Profile : 
 
 
 Dr. Mithilesh Kumar Chaube is an Assistant Professor of Mathematics in Humanities & Social Science discipline. Prior to joining IIIT-NR, he was an Assistant Professor in RKGIT Ghaziabad, Galgotias University, Greater Noida and Echelon Institute of Technology, Faridabad. He has qualified CSIR-UGC NET and was a recipient of MHRD IIT-BHU scholarship assistance during his Ph.D. tenure.He has published several journal papers in journals of international repute.

title: Board
content:
Dhananjai Pandey (Chairperson) 
 
 
 
 A Professor Dhananjai Pandey is an internationally renowned educator and researcher in the field of Materials Science and Technology. His professional career started in 1977 as a Faculty Member in BHU/IIT (BHU) and he retired as Professor (HAG) in 2017 but continued as Institute Professor for another five years. He is presently a Distinguished Professor of BHU (for life) and IIT Bhilai. 
 
 A widely travelled scientist, Prof. Pandey has delivered Invited talks/Keynote addresses/ Plenary lectures at very large number of International and National Conferences held within India and abroad. He has published over 200 research articles in journals of international repute, books and book-series with over 8000 citations (14 papers with over 100 citations) and an h-index of 49. He also holds several patents to his credit. He is a Fellow of all the three premier science academies of India (Indian National Science Academy, Indian Academy of Sciences, National Academy of Sciences of India) and a Fellow of the Asia Pacific Academy of Materials. He is a recipient of several awards/medals (INSA Young Scientist Medal, Materials Research Society of India Medal, MRSI Annual Prize and MRSI Distinguished Lectureship award, Homi Bhabha Fellowship Award, DAE Raja Ramanna Award, Pt Jubilee Award of Indian Science Congress, J C Bose National Fellowship award etc). He has
He was Coordinator (Head) of the School of Materials Science and Technology at IT,BHU, first Director in the transition phase and Dean (Faculty Affairs) of IIT BHU. Under his leadership, the School of Mater Sci & Tech emerged as an Internationally reputed Centre of research and teaching in advanced functional materials. As a Dean of Faculty Affairs, policy documents for Salary fixation, Faculty Recruitment and Promotion of deserving existing faculty members as per IIT norms were developed and successfully implemented. 
 
 
 Professor Pandey has been Member of various committees of DST, CSIR, DRDO, UGC, INSA, IASc as well as Member of Governing Body/Council and Executive Council of IIT (BHU), BHU and SU, IUAC etc. 
 
 Professor Pandey has been Vice President and President of the Indian Crystallographic Association and Vice President of the Materials Research Society of India and is currently President of the Neutron Scattering Society of India. 
 
 Professor Pandey has served as Co-editor of the leading International journals published by the International Union of Crystallography, Acta Crystallographica Section A and Journal of Applied Crystallography (first Indian), and Member of Editorial Boards of International journals (Phase Transitions, Materials Science Forum and Zeitscrift-fur-kristallographie) as well as Indian journals (Bulletin of Materials Science and Praman-a Physics Journal). He presently serves on the editorial advisory boards of Zeitscrift-fur-kristallographie and Bulletin of Materials Science. He has served on many important international bodies like Ewald Prize Committee (the highest award in crystallography) and Asia-Oceania Neutron Scattering Association (AONSA) Prize Committee, Commission on Aperiodic Crystals, Commission on Inorganic Materials and Minerals, Commission on Mathematical and Theoretical Crystallography (MaThCryst), Sub-Committee on Nomenclature for Phase Transitions of the International Union of Crystallography (IUCr). He also served as Chairman of the MaThCryst Commission of the IUCr, being only the third Indian to be elected as Chairman of any one of the IUCr Commissions over the last seven decades. He is a founding Member of EC of AONSA and continues to be its Member from India at present. 
 
 
 
 
 Sujit Baksi 
 
 
 
 Sujit Baksi is an accomplished business leader with over 30 years of experience in the IT industry. A stalwart in the areas of people management, operations management and corporate strategy, Mr. Baksi is President, Corporate Affairs, at Tech Mahindra. His track record includes being President - Global Operations, Customer Services and CEO, HCL Technologies (BPO Solutions and Services). 
 
 Mr. Baksi is a graduate of Presidency College, Kolkata and holds a Master's Degree in Personnel Management from XLRI, Jamshedpur. 
 
 
 
 
 Steven A. Pinto 
 
 
 
 Steven A. Pinto is a retired Lead Director on the Chairman's Supervisory Board of the Emirates Investment Bank, UAE. He is currently serving on the Boards of ACGL, a Tata Motors subsidiary, Easyaccess Finance, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Redington India, Adlabs Entertainment Limited and CRIF High Mark. In the year 2000, he was appointed CEO of Osool Finance Company and concurrently Head of Retail Banking at Mashreq Bank, Dubai. 
 
 He is an Economics Honors graduate with a Masters in Management from the University of Mumbai. 
 
 
 
 
 Uday B. Desai 
 
 
 
 The Founding Director of IIT Hyderabad from June 2009 to June 2019, Professor Uday B. Desai has mentored the establishment and growth of several new premier technical institutes of the nation, including IIT Bhilai, IIIT Chittoor, Sri City, etc. At present he is Prof. Emeritus, IIT Hyderabad, Honorary Professor Woosong University, South Korea, and the Chancellor of Anurag University, Hyderabad. He also serves as a Strategic Consultant for TSDSI (Telecom Standards Development Society of India), a Senior Advisor at Talent Sprint, and an Academic Consultant for Plaksha University, Punjab, India. 
 
 Prof. Desai’s academic and research experience spans over four decades in top Indian and foreign technical institutes. From 1979 to 1984 he was an Assistant Professor in the School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at Washington State University, Pullman, WA, U.S.A., and an Associate Professor at the same place from 1984 to 1987. From 1987 to May 2009 he was a Professor at IIT Bombay. He has held visiting Associate Professor Positions at Arizona State University, Purdue University, and Stanford University, U.S.A. He was a visiting Professor at EPFL (a University based in Lausanne, Switzerland) during the summer of 2002. From July 2002 to June 2004 he was the Director of HP-IITM R&D Lab. His research interests are in wireless communication, wireless sensor networks, cyber physical systems, Internet of Things and Artificial Intelligence. 
 
 Prof. Desai has several national and international awards to his credit. He received the outstanding Alumni Award from University of Buffalo (State University of New York) in 2015, and the Distinguished Alumni Award from IIT Kanpur in 2016. He is a Fellow of INSA (Indian National Science Academy), Fellow of Indian National Academy of Engineering (INAE), and a Fellow of The Institution of Electronic & Telecommunication Engineers (IETE). He is the recipient of J C Bose Fellowship. He is also the recipient of the Excellence in Teaching Award from IIT-Bombay for 2007. He is on the Board of Tata Communications Limited, and the Hyderabad Eye Research Foundation Board of LV Prasad Eye Institute. He was on the Strategy Advisory Board of Microsoft Research Lab. India. He is a member of many Central Government Committees and several Governing Councils of leading academic institutions. He Chairs several committees under MEITY (Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, India), DST (Department of Science and Technology, India), DRDO (Defense Research and Development Organization, India), and various private organizations. He is one of the founding members of COMSNETS (a premier international conference dedicated to advances in Networking and Communications Systems), and also Society for Cancer Research and Communication. He was the Chair for IEEE Bombay Section during 2006-2008. He was also on the Visitation Panel for University of Ghana. 
 
 Prof. Uday B. Desai received his B. Tech. degree from Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur, India, in 1974, the M.S. degree from the State University of New York, Buffalo, in 1976, and the Ph.D. degree from The Johns Hopkins University, Baltimore, U.S.A., in 1979. 

Prof. Desai continues to offer his services in various capacities to many technical institutes and research organizations towards his passion for nation building.
 
 
 
 
 Ankit Anand 
 
 
 
 Shri Ankit Anand is an officer of the Indian Administrative Service (IAS) belonging to the Batch of 2006 borne on Chhattisgarh Cadre. He did his B. Tech (Computer Science & Engineering) from Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kanpur. After joining the IAS, he has held several charges such as Assistant Collector Dantewada; Sub-Divisional Officer (C) Dantewada; Chief Executive Officer, Zila Panchayat, Kanker & Raipur; Collector, Jashpur, Bastar & Durg District; Chief Executive Officer, CREDA (Chhattisgarh State Renewable Energy Development Agency); Managing Director, Chhattisgarh State Power Distribution Company Limited (CSPDCL); CEO, Nava Raipur Atal Nagar Vikash Pradhikaran; Managing Director, Chhattisgarh State Marketing Federation (Markfed); Special Secretary (IC), Govt. of Chhattisgarh, Housing & Environment Department; Secretary, Govt. of C.G., Energy Department & Chairman, Chhattisgarh Power Company; Secretary to Hon'ble Chief Minister; Secretary, Govt. of C.G., Finance, Planning Economics & Statistics Department; Secretary, Govt. of C.G., Commerce & Industries Department; Secretary, Planning, Economics & Statistics Department 
 
 At Present he is Secretary, Govt. of C.G., Housing & Environment, Information & Technology, Commercial Tax (Registration); Chairman, Chhattisgarh Environment Conservation Board; and Chairman, Nava Raipur Atal Nagar Vikash Pradhikaran 
 
 
 
 
 Solai Bharathi Dasan 
 
 
 
 Dr. Solai Bharathi Dasan is an Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer of 2006 batch and is currently the Secretary, D/o Skill Development, Technical Education & Employment, Government of Chhattisgarh. He has a Doctorate degree in Agriculture. After joining IAS, he has served in several key administrative positions like Collector Surajpur; Collector Janjgir-Champa; Additional CEO (Elections); Managing Director (Civil Supplies / Consumer Affairs, Food & PD); Managing Director (Agricultural Marketing / Agriculture & Cooperation); Collector & District Magistrate, Raipur etc. 
 
 
 
 
 Vijay Dayaram K 
 
 
 
 Shri Vijay Dayaram K. is an Indian Administrative Officer of 2015 batch and is currently the Director, Technical Education, Employment and Training, Government of Chhattisgarh. Prior to this he has handled important portfolios like MD National Health Mission, Collector Bastar, Collector Balrampur, Commissioner Ambikapur Nagar Nigam, Kawardha District Panchayat CEO, Dhamtari District Panchayat CEO, Additional Collector Bilaspur, SDM Wadrafnagar, SDM Ramanujganj, and Assistant Collector of Rajnandgoan district. 
 
 
 
 
 Neeraj Jalota 
 
 
 
 Shri Neeraj Jalota is a senior power sector professional with over 37 years of distinguished experience encompassing project development, operations, maintenance, and coal mining. He holds an M.Tech in Industrial Engineering from IIT Delhi and a Post Graduate Diploma in Business Management from MDI Gurgaon, reflecting a strong blend of technical depth and managerial leadership. 
 
 Currently, Shri Jalota serves as the Executive Director, Unified Shared Services Centre (USSC), an integrated single-window platform responsible for materials procurement and contract management for NTPC stations nationwide. In this role, he has played a pivotal part in driving process standardization, operational efficiency, and enterprise-wide integration of shared services.
In addition, he holds the responsibility of Regional Executive Director (WR-II), as the administrative head of five major thermal power stations located at Korba, Sipat, and Lara in Chhattisgarh, and Gadarwara and Khargone in Madhya Pradesh. His mandate encompasses oversight of Operations and Maintenance activities for an aggregate operating capacity of 10,100 MW, as well as strategic supervision of ongoing capacity expansion projects totaling 4,000 MW, including 1,600 MW each at Lara and Gadarwara, and 800 MW at Sipat.
 
 
 Prior to his current roles, Shri Jalota served as Chief Executive Officer of the Jhabua Thermal Power Plant, where he successfully transformed the captive power plant into a profitable operation, strengthened environmental compliance, and enhanced overall operational performance.
He has undergone several in-house and overseas training programmes in technical, managerial, and mining domains. Shri Jalota’s vast leadership experience, institutional perspective, and strategic insight contribute meaningfully to governance and capacity-building initiatives.
 
 
 
 
 
 Subhash Thakur 
 
 
 
 
Shri Subhash Thakur, General Manager is the Head of Project of NTPC Limited, Lara Super Thermal Power Station, located at Raigarh District of Chhattisgarh. He brings with him 32 years of experience and an expertise in the power sector, with dexterity in diversified fields. 
 
 In his exemplary career, he has performed multi-dimensional roles in the field of Power Generation, including Operation & Maintenance, Project Engineering, Centre for Power Efficiency & Environmental Protection in projects/stations of NTPC located at Rihand, CC-EOC, Darlipali, Koldam Hydro project and Lara. He has rendered his remarkable finesse in the Operation & Maintenance of various power projects spread across all parts of India. Before coming to Lara he was Head of Project of NTPC - Koldam. 
 
 Shri Subhash Thakur is firmly committed to his responsibility towards constantly striving to set new benchmarks of efficiency and Operation Management of the power stations. With benevolence at heart, Shri Subhash Thakur is philanthropic and is actively involved in various social welfare activities as well. 
 
 
 
 
 Rachana Singh Bhal 
 
 
 
 
Ms Rachana Singh Bhal is an HR professional having 34 years of work experience in NTPC, the leading power producer in India and one of the best employers. 
 
 She is an Electronics and Instrumentation Engineer with an MBA degree. 
 
 Her experience includes Corporate Engineering, Plant Maintenance and various functions in HR domain. She has been Head of HR in various projects of NTPC. She is currently heading the Power Management Institute, the apex Learning and Development Centre of NTPC. 
 
 
 
 
 Om Prakash Vyas Member Secretary 
 
 
 
 Prof. O.P. Vyas has recently been appointed as the Director of Dr. Shyama Prasad Mukherjee International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Naya Raipur. He is a senior academician, researcher, and institutional leader with over three decades of rich experience in higher technical education, research, and academic administration. 
 
 Prof. Vyas holds an M.Tech. in Computer Science from IIT Kharagpur, and pursued his Ph.D. research at TU Kaiserslautern, Germany, under the prestigious DAAD Fellowship program. He has contributed significantly during his tenure as Dean of Technology Development, Professor of Information Technology at IIIT Allahabad, where he played a key role in academic innovation, including the conceptualization and launch of the B.Tech. (IT–Business Informatics) program. 
 
 His areas of specialization include Data Science, Process Intelligence, Business Informatics, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) and Cyber Security. He has successfully led several national and international collaborative research projects, and served as Visiting Professor in countries such as France, Germany, Italy, Norway, and Japan. 
 
 Known for his academic vision, dedication to student-centric learning, and strategic leadership, Prof. Vyas aims to elevate IIIT Naya Raipur as a premier hub of technology, innovation, and multidisciplinary education aligned with the goals of India’s National Education Policy (NEP) 2020., while also contributing meaningfully to the state's technological growth. 
 
 A native of Raipur, Chhattisgarh, Prof. Vyas is widely regarded as a pioneer of IT education in the region. As the Founding Head of the Department of Computer Science at Pt. Ravishankar Shukla University, Raipur, he introduced the region’s first formal academic program in Computer Science in 1992. This landmark initiative laid the groundwork for the expansion of IT education across Chhattisgarh. Committed to inclusive growth, Prof. Vyas worked tirelessly to extend the reach of IT education to rural and remote areas of the state, bridging the digital divide and empowering underserved communities

title: Ecosystem Partners
content:
Collaborations and ecosystems play a vital role in defining tomorrow as technology and our day-to-day lives become interconnected. To fully explore application potential and reap benefits of knowledge, partners who bring in different perspectives, diverse needs and need custom solutions are imperative. 
 
 Understanding the need for such active innovation ecosystems, IIITNR will be the hub of such energy. Partnering with other academic institutions, industry, Government and the community at-large are key aspects of the vision of IIITNR to foster a spirit of applied research and entrepreneurship. The Institute will also be launching its Centre of Innovation and Incubation, shortly, to stimulate the spirit within and outside the Institute.

title: Industry
content:
﻿ 
 
 IIIT-NR has been established with seed support of NTPC and will actively work with NTPC to develop custom solutions for their needs.Discussions with R&D teams at NTPC to understand their needs and effort to engage faculty, students of IIIT-NR in addressing those needs are moving at a swift pace. 
 
 IIIT-NR will leverage its experience in working with NTPC to forge more such partnerships with PSUs and private industry in the country going forward.

title: Government
content:
﻿ 
 Atal Nagar is gearing up to be the first Smart City in the country with excellent infrastructure already in place. IIITNR is ideally poised to become the invention hub and test bed for technologies that will enable Smart Cities in the country. IIITNR is actively seeking collaboration with the Atal Nagar Vikas Pradhikaran Authority (ANVP) to aid in the growth of the city.

 
 The Government of Chhattisgarh is also looking forward to utilizing the expertise of faculty members of IIITNR in various digitization projects within the State.

title: Academics
content:
﻿ 
 
 IIIT Bangalore has kindly agreed to mentor IIITNR. This relationship will not only provide IIIT-NR access to the best faculty of IIITB, but will also help in learning how best to interface with industry towards encouraging demand-driven innovation. 
 
 Naya Raipur is home to prestigious institutions such as the Indian Institute of Management, the National Institute of Technology, the All India Institute of Medical Sciences, the Hidayatullah National Law University, and the Indian Institute of Technology. IIIT-NR has been actively engaging with these Institutions to collaborate and establish forums for exchange of knowledge, faculty, students towards applied research and promotion of entrepreneurship in the region. Specifics of such efforts to be launched will be announced shortly.

title: Fractal Academics
content:
The recent developments in the subject areas along with the innovation in Course structuring like ‘Fractal Academics’ and ‘Skill Orientation’ were incorporated in the Curriculum design in IIIT Naya Raipur. The innovative idea of ‘Fractal Academics’ though originated from Carnegie Melon University USA is being implemented in some of the new IITs in India as well. 
 
 IIIT NR has embedded issues like ‘Job-Orientation’ and ‘Research Enabling’ as the driving force behind the Curriculum development, while encouraging the entrepreneurial instinct in the students. 
 
 The IIITNR Senate proposed a design of such curriculum which while making students Industry ready, also enables them to get into “Undergraduate Researching”.  It is being endeavored to encourage the students in their Minor and Major project work to undertake research-oriented problems so that they may get exposed to many of the prevailing Science and Technology problems while supporting Faculty members in their Project/Research work as well. 
 
 Fractal academic supports to offer a course with less credit, minimum of one credit instead of courses with large credit in conventional curriculum system. This smaller credit subject is very useful to introduce industry related emerging subject. It facilitates T-Education model for both breadth and depth of knowledge. 
 
 The academics at IIIT Naya Raipur provides an optimum mixture of academics, research, and industry. We are always eager to learn and try to incorporate new things in our flexible curriculum. 
 
 The salient features of the Curriculum of IIITNR are: 
 
 Skill First, Theory Later, Projects Integrated 
 
 Fractal Academics, that enables offering of courses with smaller credits 
 
 Learning by Doing Model 
 
 Fosters project-based learning 
 
 Exposure to both hardware + software in IT Workshops 
 
 Allows learning by application of theory 
 
 Enables development of both professional skills + technical skills 
 
 Fosters interdisciplinary skills and wider knowledge enrichment 
 
 Enables to cope up with fast changing IT revolution 
 
 Facilitates T-Education model for both breadth and depth of knowledge 
 
 Engineering courses right from First semester instead of 12th ++ courses in First year 
 
  High Industry-linkages

title: B.Tech curriculum
content:
DSPM-IIITNR currently runs three undergraduate programs: B.Tech. in Computer Science & Engineering (CSE) , B.Tech. in Electronics & Communication Engineering (ECE) and  B.Tech. in  Data Science and Artificial Intelligence (DSAI) . These credit-based programs are designed to foster core skills with innovation and entrepreneurship skills among students. The dynamic structure of the curriculum orchestrates with the changing needs of industry and academia. IIIT Naya Raipur follows the Fractal Curriculum .

title: M.Tech curriculum
content:
M.Tech. program of the Institute is open to all Indian Nationals in the following specializations: 
 
 Computer Science and Engineering (Intake :20 seats) 
 
                       Specializations: (a) Data Science and Artificial Intelligence 
 
                                                    (b) Information Security 
 
 Electronics and Communication Engineering (Intake :20 seats) 
 
                        Specializations: (a) Communication and Signal Processing 
 
                                 (b) VLSI and Embedded System 
 
 Some key features of M. Tech. program in IIIT-NR are listed below. 
 
 IIIT-NR is mentored by a highly eminent board consisting of Mr. Ajai Chowdhry (Founder-HCL), Prof. Arogyaswami Paulraj (Marconi Award winner & Professor Emeritus-Stanford University) and other visionaries from Academia and Industry. 
 
 IIIT-NR is committed to bringing to the Institute the best of the faculty and resources to enable students create solutions for today’s problems and products for the needs of the country, esp. the underprivileged. 
 
 Admissions in M.Tech. program is strictly based on valid GATE score. 
 
 Eligible Applicants admitted to M.Tech. programme will receive a monthly Institute scholarship of Rs.12,400/-. 
 
 Students are encouraged to present research papers at reputed conferences and for which the institute supports financially as per the norms of IIIT Naya Raipur. 
 
 One Semester research abroad with institute financial assistantship up to 5 Lakhs for selected candidates.

title: Ph.D
content:
IIIT Naya Raipur offers admission to the Ph.D. Programs in CSE, ECE,DSAI, Physics, Mathematics and Humanities . The goal of the Ph.D. program at IIIT-NR is to prepare the scholars for becoming global academic, industrial and R&D leaders. 
 
 Some key features of Ph.D. program in IIIT-NR are listed below. 
 
 IIIT-NR emphasizes to align its R&D focus to achieve the goal of technological self-reliance. Faculties and research scholars conduct research projects in several emerging areas of engineering, Science & Technology, and Management etc. The institute has ongoing academic and research collaborations with many national and international universities, governments and industries in order to keep pace with expanding frontiers of knowledge and global developments. 
 
 Research scholars are encouraged to propose product or service based ideas useful to society and are encouraged to build working prototypes of these products. The institute involves exploration of some of the impacts of new technologies on society, usually the unprivileged ones. 
 
 Research scholars admitted to Ph.D. program under regular Institute Scholar scheme (full time) are eligible for the Institute Teaching/Research Assistantship. 
 
 Research scholars are encouraged to present research papers at reputed conferences and attend eminent workshops in their respective research area. For this, the institute supports financially as per the norms of IIIT Naya Raipur.

title: Convocation 2023
content:
List of Degree Recipients 
 
 The 3rd Convocation of Dr. SPM IIIT-Naya Raipur is tentatively scheduled on  April 24, 2023 (Monday). Registration link for convocation will open on 5th April 2023 (8:00 PM) 
 
 Registration closes on 16th April 2023 at Midnight 
 
 
 
 Chief Guest: Shri Biswabhusan Harichandan,  
 
 Hon’ble Governor of Chhattisgarh & Chancellor of IIIT-NR 
 
 
 
 
 
 Special Guest of Honor:  Shri Bhupesh Baghel, 
 
 Hon’ble Chief Minister of Chhattisgarh  
 
 
 
 
 
 Guest of Honor:   Shri Umesh Patel, 
 
 Hon’ble Minister of Technical Education, Chhattisgarh  
 
 
 
 
 
 Guest of Honor:  Dr.  Sudhir Mishra 
 
 Ex-Director General , DRDO,Ministry of Defence, 
 
 Ex-CEO, Brahmos Aerospace, New Delhi 
 
 
 
 Important Instructions:-  
 
 Convocation will be in physical Mode only, a maximum of 02 guests/ accompany of the degree recipients will be allowed. 
 
 It is mandatory for all degree recipients to attend rehearsal on 23 April 2023. 
 
 Please enter the Convocation Venue with the proper dress code as prescribed and occupy the seat allotted to you 30 minutes before starting time. The dress codes are to be strictly followed failing which you may not be allowed to receive the degree on the dais 
 
 .  Important Dates:-  
 
  Closing of registrations 16th April 2023   11:59 pm 
 
  Rehearsal: 23 April 2023   5:00 pm 
 
 3rd Convocation 24 April 2023 11:00 am

title: Convocation 2021
content:
The 2nd  Convocation of Dr. SPM IIIT-Naya Raipur was held on November 27, 2021 (Saturday). 
 
 
 
 Chief Guest:  Miss Anusuiya Uikey,  
 
 Hon’ble Governor of CG & Chancellor of IIITNR 
 
 
 
 
 
 Special Guest of Honor: Shri Bhupesh Baghel, 
 
 Hon’ble Chief Minister of Chhattisgarh  
 
 
 
 
 
 Guests of Honor:  Shri Umesh Patel, 
 
 Hon’ble Minister of Tech. Edu. , Chhattisgarh  
 
 
 
 
 
 Guests of Honor:  Prof. Damodar Acharya 
 
  Ex. Director, IIT  Kharagpur , Ex Chairman AICTE. 
 
 
 
 Important Instructions:-  
 
 Convocation will be in physical Mode only and following the present COVID-guidelines, no guests/ accompany of the degree recipients will be allowed. 
 
 Follow the COVID-protocols like wearing masks, social distancing, etc.  
 
 It is mandatory for all degree recipients to attend rehearsal on 26th November 2021. 
 
 Please enter the Convocation Venue with the proper dress code as prescribed and occupy the seat allotted to you 30 minutes before starting time. The dress codes are to be strictly followed failing which you will not be allowed to receive the degree on the dais.  
 
 Important Dates:-  
 
  Closing of registrations  
 18th November 2021   6:00 pm 
 
  Rehearsal: 
 26th November 2021   3:00 pm 
 
  2nd Convocation 
 27th November 2021

title: Adjunct Faculty
content:
Dr. Subhas Ganguly 
 He did his Ph.D. from Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology (IIEST), Shibpur. He is presently working as Assistant Professor at National Institute of Technology, Raipur in the Department of Metallurgical Engineering. He is also an entrepreneur educator and engages in advance course in entrepreneurship. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Dr. Sheshadri Chatterjee 
 
 He did his Ph.D. and MBA from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi. Dr. Sheshadri has 12+ years of work experience in various global organizations such as IBM Global Business Services, Hewlett Packard Company, Royal Dutch Shell etc. Currently, he is working at Microsoft Corporation as Asia Pacific Regional Solution Manager. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 CA Prannay P Jain 
 Post qualification experience of about 4 years in finance, audit, taxation and company matters. Currently heading the finance department and independent director of Applylegal.com (Legal Entity Easylegal Solutions Private Limited-Mumbai) 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 CA Rajesh Kumar Gupta 
 
 Post qualification experience of about 7+ years in finance, audit and taxation. Recognized as Fellow Chartered Accountant. Currently, he is partner in a chartered accounting firm based in Raipur. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Prof. Pawan Kumar Modi 
 He started his professional career as an entrepreneur by manufacturing television and antenna in 1987. In 1992, he joined as Lecturer at the Department of Electrical Engineering, University College of Engineering, Burla Odisha (Established by Govt of Odisha in 1956). 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Prof. K K Biswas 
 
 He did His Ph.D. from IIT Delhi in the year 1973. He started teaching as a lecturer at IIT-Delhi in the year 1974 and became a Professor in the year 1988. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Dr. Nanjangud C Narendra (NCN) 
 
 Dr. Nanjangud C Narendra (NCN) is a Principal Engineer in Ericsson Research Bangalore. Prior to joining Ericsson, he worked in Cognizant Technologies, IBM Research, HP India and Motorola India. He has about 30 years R&D experience in the Indian IT industry. 
 
 
 
 
 
 Dr. Jagadish M 
 
 Dr. Jagadish M completed his Postdoctoral Research work at Chennai Mathematical Institute. He completed his M.Tech and PhD from IIT Bombay. He is currently working as a Data Analyst at Centre for Networked Intelligence, RBCCPS-EECS, IISc Bangalore. Prior to this he was an ML Instructor at Jedi Technologies Pvt. Ltd., Bengaluru.

title: Emeritus Visits
content:
Padma Bhushan Prof. Arogyaswami Paulraj 
 
 
 
 Fellow- IEEE, Fellow- Institution of Engineers, Fellow-The World Academy of Sciences, Fellow-Marconi Society, Fellow-Institution of Electronics and Telecom Engineers, India. Prof. Paulraj is an Emeritus Professor, Department of Electrical Engineering at Stanford University and a Pioneer in MIMO Wireless. He has graduated 50 doctoral and post-doctoral scholars at Stanford. 
 
 Prof. Paulraj served in the Indian Navy from 1965 to 1991, during which period, he headed the APSOH sonar program, one of India’s most successful military R&D projects. He also founded three national level labs in Artificial Intelligence and Robotics, High Performance Computing and Military Electronics. After joining Stanford in 1991, Paulraj founded Iospan Wireless Inc. (1998) which pioneered MIMO-OFDMA used in 4G, co-founded Beceem Communications Inc. (2004) which became the leader in 4G / WIMAX chip sets and co-founded Rasa Networks (2014) that developed AI tools for WiFi management. 
 
 Companies & Laboratories founded by Prof. Paulraj:- 
 
 
 
 2004 
 Beceem Comm. Inc., San Jose 
 Co-Founder 
 
 
 
 1998 
 Iospan Wireless Inc., San Jose 
 Founder 
 
 
 
 1988 
 Central Research Lab, BEL, Bangalore 
 Founding Director 
 
 
 
 1987 
 CDAC, Bangalore 
 Founding Director 
 
 
 
 1986 
 Centre for Artificial Intelligence and Robotics, Bangalore 
 Founding Director 
 
 
 
 Prof. Paulraj authored 2 Books, 6 Edited Books/Proceedings, 18 Book Chapters, 143 Journal Papers, 298 Refereed Conference Papers and 63 Issued US Patents. 
 
 Prof. Paulraj's recognitions include the 2014 Marconi Prize and the 2011 IEEE Alexander Graham Bell medal, Chief of naval Staff Medal, Vishisht Seva Medal, V K Jain Memorial Gold Medal, Scientist of the Year (1985), Technology Leadership Award, Pan IIT Council to name a few. He is a member of eight National Academies including those of USA, China, India and Sweden. 
 
 
 
 
 Prof. L.M. Patnaik 
 
 
 
 Prof. L.M. Patnaik is currently an Honorary Professor Department of Electronic Systems Engineering (formerly CEDT) at Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore. During March 2008 - August 2011, he was the Vice Chancellor, Defence Institute of Advanced Technology, Deemed University, Pune. Prior to this he was a Professor with the Department of Computer Science and Automation at the Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore. He obtained his Ph.D in 1978 in the area of Real-Time Systems, D.Sc. in 1989 in the areas of Computer Systems and Architectures, both from the Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore 
 
 He is Fellow of the IEEE, The Academy of Sciences for the Developing World (TWAS), The Computer Society of India, Indian National Science Academy, Indian Academy of Sciences, National Academy of Sciences, and Indian National Academy of Engineering. He is a Distinguished Lecturer of the IEEE Region 10 and a distinguished Visitor of the IEEE Computer Society for the Asia-Pacific Region. 
 
 He has published over 700 papers in refereed International Journals and refereed International Conference Proceedings and authored 27 technical reports. He is a co-editor/co-author of 21 books and authored 12 chapters in other books in the areas of VLSI System Design and Parallel Computing, He has supervised over 30 Doctoral theses and over 160 Masters theses in the above areas. 
 
 As a recognition of his contributions in the areas of Electronics, Informatics, Telematics and Automation, he was awarded the Dr. Vikram Sarabhai Research Award in 1989,The VASVIK Award in the Field of Electronic Sciences and Technology, Vividhlaxi Audyogik Samshodhan Vikas Kendra,1996, The Fourth Sir C V Raman Memorial Lecture Award in 2000, The IEEE Computer Society Meritorious Service Award, 2002, Indian Science Congress Association Srinivasa Ramanujan Birth Centenary Award, 2007-2008 and Distinguished Alumnus Award, 2012, National Institute of Technology, Rourkela to name a few of the many awards to his name.

title: CoE in Next Generation Network
content:
In recent times, there have been many disruptive technologies such as mobile phones, the Internet, 3D printing, Blockchain, etc. A disruptive technology brings transformational changes to our society by displacing conventional methods of doing things by creating a completely new industry. The 5th generation mobile network (5G) is another disruptive technology, which is yet to penetrate our society. As compared to mobile networks of previous generations (2G, 3G and 4G), 5G based mobile networks will interconnect not only people, but will also interconnect smart machines, objects, and devices. Data communication speed of 5G mobile networks is expected to be 35 times faster than that of 4G mobile networks. Due to the high speed of 5G mobile networks, users of 5G-enabled mobile devices will be able to use them for interactive multimedia applications, Internet-of-Things (IoT) applications, High Definition quality mobile TV (HDTV), video conferencing with mobile devices, and many other new types of mobile applications. 
 
 To promote research and innovation in this highly promising area, IIIT Nava Raipur has setup a 5G testbed in its Centre of Excellence (CoE) in Next Generation Networks. The CoE has been established in line with Govt. of India’s goal to build indigenous testbeds that closely resemble real-world 5G deployments. This will encourage Indian startups and industries to take an early lead in 5G technology. This testbed is first of its kind not only in Chhattisgarh but in Central India. IIIT-NR is now among the few premier institutions in India like IIT-Madras, IISc-Bangalore, IIT-Kanpur, IIT-Delhi, IIT-Hyderabad and SAMEER-Mumbai, which have their own 5G testbed.

 Dr. Rajarshi Mahapatra

title: Ph.D Scholars
content:
Aakanksha Baghel ( Electronics and Communication Engineering ) 
 
 
 
 Aakanksha Baghel completed her B.Tech. in Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering from Shri Shankaracharya Technical Campus, Bhilai, India (2018), and her M.Tech. in Microelectronics and VLSI from UTD, CSVTU, Bhilai, India (2022). She has two years of industry experience (2018–2020) and currently serves as a Junior Research Fellow (JRF) while actively pursuing a Ph.D. at IIIT, Naya Raipur, Chhattisgarh.

Her research interests include Molecular Communication and Targeted Drug Delivery, with a focus on developing innovative solutions at the intersection of biomedical engineering and communication technologies. 

 
 Supervisor: Prof. Rajarshi Mahapatra, Co-supervisor: Dr. Debanjan Das 
 
 
 
 
 Abhishek Shrivastava ( Computer Science and Engineering ) 
 
 
 
 Abhishek Shrivastava completed his B.E. in Information Technology from GEC Raipur and M.Tech in Computer Technology from NIT Raipur. He has worked as an Assistant Professor in various engineering colleges for the past 16 years. His areas of interest include Myocardial Infarction, Signal Processing, Medical Image Analysis, Transfer Learning, and IoMT. Research Article Published: "Quantum leap in cardiac prognosis: EMIP-cardioPPG’s pioneering approach to early myocardial infarction prediction" in Journal of Signal Image and Video processing 2024 and " A novel hybrid model for predictive analysis of myocardial infarction using advanced machine learning techniques" 2023 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT). 
 Supervisor	:- Dr. Santosh Kumar , Co-Supervisor :- Dr. Nenavath Srinivas Naik 
 
 
 
 
 Amar Sinha ( COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING ) 
 
 
 
 Amar Sinha received his B.Tech. degree in Computer Science and Engineering from Maulana Abul Kalam Azad University of Technology (formerly WBUT), West Bengal, India, in 2020. He subsequently earned his M.Tech. degree in Computer Science with a specialization in Cyber Physical Systems (CPS) from the Central University of Rajasthan, Ajmer, India, in 2022. He then served as a Junior Research Fellow (JRF) for three years and is presently pursuing a Ph.D. degree in Computer Science and Engineering at IIIT Naya Raipur, Chhattisgarh, India. 
 
 His research interests focus on AI/ML algorithms for mobility management, Software-Defined Networking (SDN), and the Internet of Things (IoT). 
 
 Supervisor: Dr. Mallikharjuna Rao K 
 
 
 
 
 Ambika Prasad Choubey ( Management ) 
 
 
 
 Ambika Prasad Choubey holds a Master of Business Administration degree with a specialization in Human Resource Development & Information Technology. He has over two decades of diverse experience spanning a broad spectrum of administration and personnel management functions, having served in premier government organizations including Institutions of National Importance, as well as in the industry both in India and internationally. 
His research interests include talent acquisition and workplace well-being.
 

 
 
 Supervisor: Dr. Amit Kumar Agrawal. 
 
 
 
 
 
 Anjali Manhas ( English ) 
 
 
 
 Anjali Manhas has completed her Bachelors in Science in the year 2017 from GCW
Parade, Jammu and Masters in English in the year 2019 from Shri Mata Vaishno Devi
University, Katra, Jammu. She qualified for the UGC NET in English in December 2019 and GATE in English(2021) with AIR of 293. She served as a guest faculty in the Department of English at GCW Parade, Jammu from 2021 to 2022.
Her research interests include Gender and Cultural Studies, Medical Humanities, Film Studies, and Literary Theory. Currently, she is working on the representation of abortion narratives and reproductive rights of women in literature under the supervision of Dr. Sresha Yadav Nee Ghosh. 
 

 
 
 Supervisor: Dr. Sresha Yadav Nee Ghosh 
 
 
 
 
 
 Chanchal Ijardar ( Physics ) 
 
 
 
 Chanchal Ijardar has completed her Bachelor's and Master's in Electronics (Honors) from Guru Ghasidas University, Bilaspur in the year 2019 and 2021, respectively. She has qualified for the UGC NET in July 2023 and joined PhD in January 2024. Currently, she is pursuing PhD under the supervision of Dr. Punya Prasanna Paltani and co-supervision of Dr. Manoj Kumar Majumder. Her current research interest include defects formed during the fabrication of ICs, such as pinholes and voids in 3D Integrated Circuits. 
 
 
 

 
 
 Supervisor:Dr. Punya Prasanna Paltani , Co- Supervisor: Dr. Manoj Kumar Majumder 
 
 
 
 
 
 D. Venkata Siva Prasad ( Electronics and Communication Engineering ) 
 
 
 
 D. Venkata Siva Prasad has Joined Ph.D. in ECE on 03 September 2020 in IIIT Naya Raipur under the supervision of Dr. Shrivishal Tripathi, Assistant Professor, IIIT Naya Raipur. He has completed his B.Tech in Electronics and Communications Engineering from KSRMCE, Kadapa, Andhra Pradesh, in 2008 and M.Tech in DECS from JNTUCE, Ananthapur, Andhra Pradesh, in 2014. He worked as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, in BVRIT Hyderabad College of Engineering for Women from January-2017 to July-2020. He is a student member of IEEE and member of IAENG 
 
 His area of interest includes Full Duplex Antenna Design for 5G Communications. 
 
 Supervisor	:- Dr. Shrivishal Tripathi, Co-Supervisor :- Dr. Punya P. Paltani 
 
 Click Here For Publication Details 
 
 
 
 
 Dipanjan Sar ( Electronics and Communication Engineering ) 
 
 
 
 Dipanjan Sar is a dedicated researcher specializing in wireless communication, AI/ML, Internet of Vehicles (IoV) communication, and waveform design. He is currently pursuing his Ph.D. in Electronics and Communication Engineering at IIIT Naya Raipur, alongside working as a Senior Research Fellow (SRF) in a National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS) funded project.

With a B.Tech in ECE from West Bengal University of Technology (WBUT) and an M.Tech from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad, he brings a strong academic foundation to his research. His work focuses on channel estimation and waveform design for the next-generation Internet of Vehicles (IoV).

Dipanjan has also contributed significantly to academia, having over three years of teaching experience at Techno India Banipur (WB) and Chaibasa Engineering College (Jharkhand). His expertise lies in bridging the gap between theoretical research and practical applications, driving innovation in wireless communication and intelligent vehicular networks.

 
 Supervisor: Prof. Rajarshi Mahapatra, Co-supervisor: Dr. Debanjan Das 
 
 
 
 
 Dixit Sharma ( Electronics and Communication Engineering ) 
 
 
 
 Dixit Sharma holds a B.Tech. degree in ECE from BUEST, Baddi, Himachal Pradesh (2014), and an M.Tech. degree in Communication Engineering from VIT, Chennai, Tamil Nadu (2018). He is currently pursuing a Ph.D. degree in ECE at IIIT, Naya Raipur, since January 2023. Since December 2022, he has been a JRF at the IIITB Comet Foundation, NMICPS.

His research interests include smart radio environments, 5G and beyond wireless communication, and beam steering using intelligent reflecting surfaces. 

 
 Supervisor: Prof. Rajarshi Mahapatra, Co-supervisor: Dr. Debanjan Das 
 
 
 
 
 Gopesh Kumar Bharti ( Computer Science and Engineering ) 
 
 
 
 Gopesh Kumar Bharti is a Junior Research Fellow (JRF) at IIIT Naya Raipur and a Ph.D. scholar in Computer Science and Engineering. His research is centered on developing an AI-based translation tool for converting English/Hindi text and speech to tribal languages, particularly Gondi, and vice versa. Leveraging transformer-based neural machine translation models, his work aims to enhance linguistic inclusivity and accessibility. He holds an M.Tech and B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering from Chhattisgarh Swami Vivekanand Technical University, Bhilai (C.G.). His research contributes to the preservation and promotion of indigenous languages through AI-driven language technologies. 

 
 

 
 
 Supervisor : Dr. Santosh Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Harshita Dubey ( Electronics and Communication Engineering ) 
 
 
 
 Harshita Dubey is a research scholar pursuing Ph.D. in Machine Learning, with a focus on integrating image processing, AI, and machine learning. She holds an M.Tech in Embedded Systems from Symbiosis University, Pune, where her research explored high-security ML algorithms for Industrial IoT. She did her BE in Electronics & Communication, ME in Industrial Engineering & Management alongside distance MBA in HR and Finance. She has worked with firms like Infosys, Amazon, Paytm, Aegis, CSIR, ARAI, BSNL etc. dealing with IT Operations, IT Recruitment and Patent Mapping. With experience in patent research, embedded systems, and industrial management, she brings a multidisciplinary approach to her work. Passionate about innovation, she is committed to advancing AI-driven solutions under expert guidance. 

 
 
 Supervisor: Dr. Abhishek Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Kamal Solanki ( Electronics and Communication Engineering ) 
 
 
 
 Kamal Solanki received his B.E. in Electronics and Tele­communication Engineering from the Christian College of Engineering and Technology, Bhilai, Chhattisgarh, India 2015. and M.Tech. Degree specializing in Optoelectronics and Laser Electronics under S.O.S. in the Electronics and Photonics Department from the PTRSU Raipur, India, in 2018. He worked at the Indian Institute of Chemical Technology as a project assistant for his work on Organic and inorganic solar cells. Also, he worked as an Assistant Professor for more than 4 years. He is working towards his Ph.D. from Dr. Shyama Prasad Mukherjee International Institute of Information Technology Naya Raipur, India. His current research interests are in graphene nanoribbon-based gas sensors and emerging nanomaterials. 
Email: kamalsolanki033@gmail.com

 
 Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=rt8SimAAAAAJ&hl=en 
 
 ORCID: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4036-9350 
 Supervisor: Dr. Manoj Kumar Majumder , Co- Supervisor: Dr. Punya Prasanna Paltani 
 
 
 
 
 Kamna Sahu ( Computer Science and Engineering )

She qualified CSIR NET(LS) Dec 2019 in Physical Science and Gate 2020 in Physics.
 
 
 Her area of research includes Photonic Crystal Based Photonic Devices. 
 
 Supervisor : Dr. Punya Prasanna Paltani , Co-Supervisor: Dr. Shrivishal Tripathi

title: 3D Printing & Fabrication Lab
content:
The 3D Printing & Fabrication Lab is a state-of-the-art digital manufacturing space and allows students the opportunity to quickly fabricate parts from computer-generated designs to rapidly iterate prototype designs. Digital Fabrication is revolutionary technology that will change the way we fabricate everything, from machine parts, concrete structures, auto-parts, prosthetics, body organs, foods, printed circuit boards, to almost everything. 
 
 The lab offers access to laser cutting and engraving, CNC cutting, milling, 3D scanners and an array of 3D printers equip to tackle different designs. The lab is always buzzing with activity and new ideas proving vital for students working on projects for both class and research. It is a small scale workshop offering digital fabrication which empowers the users to create smart devices for themselves which can be tailored to local or personal needs. 
 
   
 
 
 
 Lab In-charge 
 
             Dr. Debanjan Das 
 
 Assistant Professor, ECE 
 
 E-mail:  debanjan@iiitnr.edu.in 
 
 
 
 
 
 Lab Assistant 
 
 Purnendra dhruw, 
 
 E-mail:  purnendra@iiitnr.edu.in 
 
   
 
 
 
   
 
 Resources 
 
 Hardware/Machines 
 
 
 
 Laser Cutting Machine 
 
 
 
 It a TechB co2 Laser cutting machine with power 150W, allows the area or dimension for cutting and engraving is 90 x135 cm2 and suitable for the acrylic and thin ply sheets. 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 CNC Router 
 
 
 
 A computer numeric control for cutting and engraving into 2D and 3D models .Dimension is 700 mm x1000 mmx300 mm material can be used hardwood, soft aluminum, ply woods and more. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 FDM High Temperature 3D Printer 
 
 
 
 3D printers, also known as 3D rapid-prototypers, are capable of taking digital geometry in the form of a stereolithographic file (.stl) and producing small models. One 3D printer with build volume of 300x300x350 mm3 and material support of PLA, ABS, Nylon, Flex. 
 
 
 
 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 FDM Low Temperature 3D Printer 
 
 
 
 3D printers, also known as 3D rapid-prototypers, are capable of taking digital geometry in the form of a stereolithographic file (.stl) and producing small models. One 3D printer with build volume of 300x300x350 mm3 and material support of PLA, ABS, Nylon, available for the students. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 FDM Low Temperature 3D Printer 
 
 
 
 3D printers, also known as 3D rapid-prototypers, are capable of taking digital geometry in the form of a stereolithographic file (.stl) and producing small models. One 3D printer with build volume of 200x200x250 mm3 and material support of PLA, ABS, Nylon, is available for the students. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 SLA  3D Printer 
 
 
 
 Stereolithography (SLA) is a technology that converts liquid materials into solid parts, layer by layer, by selectively curing them using a light source in a process called photopolymerization. SLA is widely used to create models, prototypes, patterns, and production parts for a range of industries from engineering and product design to manufacturing, dentistry, jewellery, model making, and education. 
 
 These printers can print an array of different material with varying properties. Materials available are: tough, durable, flexible and standard. Standard material comes in white, black, grey and clear. The print area of this machine is 150mm x 150mm x 150mm. 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Magnetic Drill Machine 
 
 
 
 A magnetic drilling machine allows a portable drill with magnetic base of permanent magnet. 
 
 
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
 
   
 
   
 
 
 
 
 B. Softwares 
 
 
 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 Cura 15.14.4 
 
 
 
 A Open source license based software, suitable for conversion of 3d model (.stl)  to machine language (.gcode) 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Rhino 6 
 
 
 
 Model any shape you can imagine. Compatibility with all your other design, drafting, CAM, engineering, analysis, rendering, animation, and illustration software. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Artcam 
 
 
 
 ArtCAM includes several minor enhancements, such as, increasing the number of shortcuts you can create for Toolbox add-ins; providing support for 4K ultra-high-definition resolution screens;  the ArtCAM interface directly from within the software; making it easier to identify active items in the Project Tree; specifying the default view displayed when creating a model; and specifying the language setting directly from within the software. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 RDWorks V8 
 
 
 
 RDWorks V8 is a powerful program that allows you to perform laser cutting engraving operations. The program has support for drawing points, lines (horizontal/vertical), polyline, ellipse/circle, rectangular/square, Bezier curve, text, and for CAD models such as DXF, AI and PLT.

title: Antenna and Microwave Circuit Design Lab
content:
Laboratory Name and Location 
 
 Antenna and Microwave Circuit Design Lab 
 
 Objective 
 
 The Microwave Lab supports intermediate and advanced courses in Electromagnetics and Microwave Engineering. Students experiment with transmission line propagation, antennas and microwave circuit components. The microwave laboratories provide the necessary support for training the students in the area of RF and Microwave Engineering. It offers analysis and simulation of various components and devices to understand the basics of RF and microwave engineering, to boost the quality of engineering education, deepen understanding, and provide the necessary practical skills to young minds.

title: AIDL
content:
Location: Room No. 210, First Floor 
  About lab: - 
  
Artificial Intelligence and Deep Leaning (AIDL) lab is a research oriented setup and started in year 2018 at IIIT Naya Raipur with focus on: 
It has been stablished to carries out work on a variety of topics within Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning with a focus on using real world problems to discover computationally hard problems of modelling uncertainty, learning predictive models and enabling decision making.  
The AID Lab brings together IIITNR researchers across the areas of computer vision, machine learning, natural language processing. 
AIDL includes over 50 students and researchers (both internal and external) pursuing research on fundamental advances in the above areas as well as cross-cutting themes including multi-modal deep learning, human-compatible AI, and connecting AI with other scientific disciplines and the humanities. 
It also provides computing platform for novel languages like Python, R, and OpenCV and other necessary for building coherent set of ideas, fundamental of Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning models and algorithms. 
Members of the AIDL lab seeks cutting edge problem-based solutions to data mining, computer vision and machine learning problems arising in numerous application areas involving data analytics.  
  Objective: Understand Intelligence: Today's AI provides nothing like the general purpose, flexible intelligence that we have as humans. AIDL aims to understand the ingredients of intelligence that enable the fast and flexible ways in which humans learn.Second Machine age: changing the world requires two things 
 
 Physical Power: move or transform the things (first machine age) Mental Power: decide where and how (second machine age)           
 
 This lab builds machines to give mental power that learn and think like people. AIDL lab facilitates various ascendable algorithms for building predictive models from large, distributed, semantically disparate data and for constructing predictive models from sequence, image, text, multi-relational, graph-structured data. 
Exploring the creation of novel statistical and computational methods for scalable data mining, machine learning, optimization as well as statistical modelling with complex data sets. 
AIDL are especially focus in machine learning, data mining and information retrieval. In all of these areas, the combination of well-informed theoretical models empowered by large-scale resources allows for exciting insights and applications.  
  
  Highlights in Lab 
Machine Leaning, Computer Vision, Deep Learning, Bioinformatics, Video Processing, NLP 
  Technical background: 
A Multi GPU Server equipped with processors are high-end 2 x Intel® Scalable processors Xeon 6148 and dual GeForce GTX 1080TI (11GB) with total SSD Storage of server 30 x 4000GB. Lab includes 10 Multi GPU workstations equipped with 2 x Intel® Scalable processors Xeon 4114 with 64GB (Max 1024GB) of memory, 8TB of disk space and dual GeForce GTX 1080TI (11GB). title: Big Data Computing Lab
content:
Location: Room No. 216, First Floor, Academic Building 
 
 About lab: 
 
 The purpose of the BigData Computing Lab is to educate students in all aspects of large and distributed information systems (e.g. system development, testing, maintenance, data integration, networking, and application development) and, thus prepare them for highly emerging technologies in fast-growing IT industries such as BigData computing, Health-care informatics, Data integration and Data analytics. The research in the lab mainly focuses on next-generation data management and mining issues on BigData with special attention to heterogeneous information networks and social computing 
 
 The Lab mainly houses necessary hardware platforms and software tools for the following domains of research in BigData analytics: 
 
             Healthcare Data Analytics 
 
             Agriculture Data Analytics 
 
             Financial Data Analytics 
 
             Computer Vision 
 
             Machine Learning 
 
 Facilities: 
 
 An experimental setup for BigData analytics platform primarily using open source technologies such as Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS), MapReduce, YARN, Hive, MongoDB, Hbase, Apache Spark, MLlib, etc. 
 
 Programming languages and software: Python, Java, R, Scala, etc. 
 
 Purpose: 
 
 To design and develop data driven solutions for improving healthcare operations and Agriculture sector 
 
 To design behavioural technologies (at the individual as well as at community level based on social networks of individuals) for preventive care 
 
 To introduce and practice case-based learning and keeping students updated with world-class cases for learning management practices
