title: Biomedical and Speech Processing Lab
content:
Lab In-charge: Dr. Anurag Singh 
 
 Email:  anurag@iiitnr.edu.in 
 
 Phone: 91-771-2474033 
 
 
 
   
 
 Overview 
 
 The laboratory focuses on the research and development activities related to speech processing, biomedical signal and image processing, cognition and language processsing areas. The lab has high performance computing workstations, GPU servers, recording and listening equipment, and data acquisition modules for several physiological signals like ECG, PPG, EEG, EMG, Respiratory rate, etc. 
 
 Vision 
 
 To be a leading research and development group in the biomedical and speech processing areas. 
 
 Area of Research 
 
 Imagined Speech processing 
 
 Pathological/Stressed Speech Processing 
 
 Signal Processing 
 
 Machine Learning and Deep learning 
 
 Cardiovascular Signal Processing 
 
 AI in Healthcare 
 
 Brain-Computer Interface 
 
 Lab Facilities: 
 
 The lab is well furnished with all the necessary equipment to perform smooth research and analysis in this domain. The lab is fully covered with wooden walls from the inside to have an ideal acoustic environment for voice recording. 
 
 Data Acquisition Devices: 
 
 MP36 Four-Channel Data Acquisition System 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 
 The MP data acquisition unit is useful for acquiring almost all physiological signals such as ECG, EMG, respiratory, PPG, skin temperature, etc. The MP Unit has an internal microprocessor to control data acquisition and communication with the computer. The MP Unit takes incoming signals and converts them into digital signals that can be processed with the computer. There are analog input channels (four on MP36 units, two on MP45), one of which can be used as a trigger input. The MP Unit must be connected to the computer, and electrodes, transducers, and/or I/O devices must be connected to the MP Unit. 
 
 ● NeuroMax Portable EEG System 
 
 The EEG channel facilitates monitoring up to 32 channels and has a USB interface; no external power or internal battery is required. It helps the brain map multiple options and facilitates dual monitor options for video. It helps in Online/ Offline split video, Online impedance check with a display of numeric values of all electrodes, and in Online/ Offline reformatting of filters, low filters, sensitivity, and sweep on channels. It has various options for EEG scrolling and is highly synchronized EEG with audio and video frame rates up to 30 frames/sec. The channel has a resolution-independent user interface, Coherence, FFT Graph, and tabular data. It acquires simultaneous analysis and acquisition of EEG data. Facility to mark the event and write a comment in analysis mode. Complete EEG data transfer on CD/DVD/ Blueray Disc. The channel has easy connectivity with laptop/ desktop, use define photic simulate protocols, a split screen facility to study any event/montage carefully during acquisition, and has an auto backup of patient data for easy recovery and remote control video tracking device. 
 
 
 
 Server PC/Workstation: 
 
 
 
 Key configuration: 
 
 Intel Xeon Gold 5218 CPU @2.30 GHz -2 Processors 
 
 128 Gb usable RAM 
 
 64-bit Operating System x64-based processor 
 
 32-cores and NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 
 
 List of PhD Scholars working in the lab: 
 
 Mr. Shresth Gupta, Research Area : PPG signal applications for blood pressure estimation, non-invasive diabetes detection, hypertension detection, etc. 
 
 Mrs. Nalini Pusarla,  Research Area : Emotion recognition using EEG signals 
 
 Ms. Anjali Manhas ,   Research Area : Speech and language processing

title: Communication Lab
content:
Welcome to the communication lab at IIIT-Naya Raipur. This communication Lab provides the necessary hardware and software resources to the undergraduate and research students of electronics and communication engineering for enhancing their skills in communication field. Theory in the classroom is translated directly into practice with the help of the Ettus USRP software defined radio platform and Matlab/National Instrument’s LabVIEW. The emphasis is on physical layer concepts rather than implementation considerations.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Dr. Anurag Singh 
 
 Lab Incharge, Assistant Professor 
 
 E-mail: anurag@iiitnr.edu.in 
 
 
 
 
 Equipments 
 
 
 
 
 . 
 Description: The Signal Analyser (Keysight MXA N9020B-513) provides a frequency range from 10 Hz to 13.6 GHz with a maximum analysis bandwidth of 160 MHz. 
 
 
 
 Description: The Digital Anolog Telecommunication EXperimenter unit (DATex) is an add-on board for the NI ELVIS used for teaching analog and digital Telecommunications theory to university students. With Emona DATEx, over 29 analog and digital telecom’s experiments can be implemented on one board, plugged into the NI ELVIS platform. These experiments include basic analog communication experiments, such as amplitude modulation (AM), frequency modulation (FM), phase modulation (PM) and digital communication experiments including sampling, pulse-code modulation (PCM), amplitude-shift keying (ASK), and quadrature phase shift keying (QPSK), frequency-shift keying (FSK) and more. In addition, it also has the PC-controlled capabilities, such as on-screen control of hardware circuit blocks running Labview, automated signal processing and analysis using ELVIS instruments and building of integrated hardware-in the-loop signal control programs with Labview controlling electronic circuit blocks. 
 
 . 
 
 
 
 
 . 
 Description:  RTL-SDR is a very cheap software defined radio that uses a DVB-T TV tuner dongle based on the RTL2832U chipset. It was found that by using some software the signal I/Q data could be accessed directly from this TV tuner, which allowed this DVB-T TV tuner to be converted into a wideband software defined radio via a new software driver. Essentially, this means that a cheap TV tuner can be used as a computer based radio scanner in the frequency range from approximately 25MHz-1750MHz. 
 
 
 
 Description: The RF hardware used in this lab is the National Instruments USRP (Universal Software Radio Peripheral). The USRP-2920 is a tunable RF transceiver (both transmit and receive communications) with a high-speed analog to digital converter and digital to analog converter for streaming baseband I and Q signals to a host PC over 1/10 Gigabit Ethernet. This device can operate in the frequency range from 50 MHz to 2.2 GHz with 20 MHz Bandwidth. This hardware can be configured using LabVIEW and GRC (GNU Radio Companion) software. You can use the USRP 2920 hardware for Wireless sensor networks; Non Orthogonal Multiple Access Simulation, Spectrum Sensing; amateur radio; GPS and MIMO, Massive MIMO simulation and realization. 
 
 . 
 
 
 
 
 . 
 Description: The USRP N200 series provides high-bandwidth, high-dynamic range processing capability. The product architecture includes a Xilinx® Spartan® 3A-DSP 1800 FPGA, 100 MS/s dual ADC, 400 MS/s dual DAC and Gigabit Ethernet connectivity to stream data to host processors. A modular design allows the USRP N200 to operate from DC to 6 GHz. An expansion port allows multiple USRP N200 series devices to be synchronized and used in a MIMO configuration. An optional GPSDO module can also be used to discipline the USRP N200 reference clock to within 0.01 ppm of the worldwide GPS standard. The USRP N200 can stream up to 50 MS/s to and from host applications, and users can implement custom functions in the FPGA fabric, or in the on-board 32-bit RISC softcore. The FPGA offers the potential to process up to 100 MHz of RF bandwidth in both transmit and receive directions. 
 
 
 
 Description: The Virtual Bench All-in-One Instrument combines a mixed-signal oscilloscope with protocol analysis, an arbitrary waveform generator, a digital multimeter, a programmable DC power supply, and digital I/O. The all-in-one features are simple, convenient, and provide more efficient circuit design, debugging, and validation. The included software lets you view all measurements on a single screen and can be updated with free software releases to add functionality and value. The VirtualBench All-in-One Instrument easily integrates with LabVIEW software to provide programmatic control and automated test sequences. 
 
 . 
 
 
 
 
 . 
 The Emona SIGEx 311 Signals and Systems Experiments Board for NI ELVIS II is an add-on application board for the NI Educational Laboratory Virtual Instrumentation Suite II (NI ELVIS II) or NI ELVIS II+. This device is defined specifically for teaching signals and systems. With the Emona SIGEx 311 Signals and Systems Experiments Board for NI ELVIS II can be effectively used to teach different concepts such as characterizing linear and nonlinear signals, understanding convolution, using poles and zeros in the Laplace domain, and sampling and aliasing with a hands-on experimental approach.

title: Computer Programming Lab
content:
“ Whether you want to uncover the secrets of universe or you just want to pursue a career in the 21st century, basic computer programming is an essential skill to learn ”    
 
  -   Stephen Hawking 
 
 Location :       Room No. 301, Second Floor, Academic Building 
 
 About Lab :    Computer Programming Lab is a learning setup which started in 2016 at IIIT NR with focus on developing the program in high level language and translation into machine level language. Computer Programming lab is a hands-on project and assignment-based learning space where students will gain strong practical and technical skills in various  programming  languages including C, Python and Java, UNIX/Linux: shell, tools, utilities and  programming  environments, user interfaces, and software engineering principles. The interactive experiments in this lab will give the students an opportunity for learning and better understanding of the basic concepts and constructs of computer programming as well as advanced methodology concepts like Deep learning, Big data Technologies, Artificial Intelligence, Computational Intelligence etc.  
 
 Objectives :   To introduce students to the basic knowledge of  programming  fundamentals of different programming languages like C, Python, and Java etc. as well as advanced programming concepts required for cutting edge programming needs towards Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Big Data, Artificial Intelligence etc. During lab sessions, the student should inculcate following key attributes: 
 
 
 
 
  Knowledge  – Should know about: 
 
 techniques for solving problems 
 
 Understand fundamentals of object-oriented programming including defining classes, invoking methods, using class libraries, etc. 
 
 basic computational concepts and elementary data structures 
 
 the edit-compile-link-run cycle from a user point of view 
 
 testing strategies 
 
 the main activities of software development and their interactions, and some of the major problems of software development 
 
   Skills  – Should be able to: 
 
 hand-execute simple programs, showing how input data is processed, output data is produced, and how the values of internal variables change 
 
 explain at various levels the behaviour of fragments of programming language code 
 
 amend existing programs to adjust or correct their functionality 
 
 translate well-structured plans into working programs 
 
 analyse simple problems involving text, numbers and graphics, producing a top-level plan with refinements 
 
 use the error messages of the compiler to identify and correct mistakes in program syntax 
 
 use testing strategies to identify and correct semantic errors in programs 
 
     Attitudes  – Should appreciate that: 
 
 a programmer requires creativity in order to solve problems and precision in the construction and manipulation of programming language code 
 
 a programmer builds up a repertoire of techniques for solving problems, usually adapting and reusing techniques as each new problem is encountered 
 
 a programmer must be able to communicate his/her ideas to others 
 
 effective programming requires effort both in front of and away from a computer 
 
 learning to program requires commitment and perseverance

title: Data Science Lab
content:
Laboratory Location: Room no: 132, Ground Floor, Academic Block 
 
 About Lab: 
 
 Data Science Lab is configured which not only facilitates students to practice basic programming in C, C++, and JAVA; but also learn advanced technologies of computer science such as Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning and Data Mining. The Lab facilitates database designing and management through dedicated tools such as Oracle, and MySQL and related computing paradigm and frameworks.  
 
 It also provides computing platform for novel languages like Python, R, and OpenCV and other necessary for building coherent set of ideas, fundamental of Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning models and algorithms. Each lab sessions is aimed to translate the theory lectures into practical implementation through programming paradigms and tools, platforms provided in the data science lab. Data Science Lab welcomes students, researchers, interns, faculties, newbies and everyone from a broad range of disciplines who are fascinated with the possibilities of turning big and complex data to knowledge. 
 
 Members of the data science lab seeks cutting edge problem-based solutions to data mining, computer vision and machine learning problems arising in numerous application areas involving data analytics. 
 
 Objective: 
 
 The long-term research agenda of the Data Science Lab is to develop new algorithms and software tools for data management and mining, and to use them for social good.  
 
 The Data Science Lab focuses on applying machine learning, data mining, and network analysis to real-world problems in society and industry. 
 
 Exploring the creation of novel statistical and computational methods for scalable data mining, machine learning, optimization as well as statistical modelling with complex data sets 
 
 We are especially interested in machine learning, data mining and information retrieval. In all of these areas, the combination of well-informed theoretical models empowered by large-scale resources allows for exciting insights and applications. 
 
   
 
 Lab Configuration:  T he Lab constitutes of total 60 desktops with following configuration: 
 
 Hardware configuration                   
 Intel i7 processor with 2.5GHz clock frequency, 8GB RAM, 500GB Hard Disk. 
 
 Operating System 
 Windows10, Ubuntu 16.04.

 Python 3.6.5, R-Language, OpenCV, C, C++, JAVA. 
 
 Licensed Tools 
 Oracle 12.1.0.2.0 with analytics.

 High Speed Internet, Projector, White Board, Intercom

title: Electronic Circuit Lab
content:
Laboratory Name and Location : Electronic Circuit Lab, Room No – 302, Academic Block, IIIT Naya Raipur, Contact No. – (0771)2474048

 
 
 OBJECTIVE:   Electronic Circuit Lab is associated with the institute core course Digital System Design, Electronic Circuits, Analog Integrated Circuits and IT Workshop (Hardware Module). This lab runs in both the semester and accommodates approximately 120 students per semester. The laboratory has basic electronic experiments on analog, digital and mixed signal circuits.

 
 
 The Primary objective of this Lab is: 
 
 
 To provide engineering skills by way of breadboard circuit design with electronic devices and components. 
 
 To design and analyse various Electronic circuits and digital circuits such as multi-vibrators, Flip- Flop circuits, applications of operational amplifiers, RC coupled amplifiers, oscillators etc. so that students are able to understand the practical aspects of basic electronics theory. 
 
  To enable the students to simulate and test the Analog, Digital and mixed Electronics circuits using Verilog, Matlab and OrCAD software.  
 
 
 List of Equipments 
 
 
 
 
 
 Major Equipment 
 
 
 
 Maker 
 
 
 
 Description 
 
 
 
 Snapshot 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Function Generator 
 
 
 
 SCIENTIFIC MES TECHNIK 
 
 
 
 It is used to generate different kind of mathematical waveforms like square, pulse, sine, tringle, saw tooth etc.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Digital Storage Oscilloscope (DSO) 
 
 
 
 SCIENTIFIC MES TECHNIK 
 
 
 
 It is used to show and measure the different kind of signals like voltage, current, power etc.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Analog –Digital Trainer kit 
 
 
 
 SCIENTECH TECHNOLOGIES PVT LTD 
 
 
 
 It is a trainer kit for making experimental set up. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 DC Power Supply 
 
 
 
 SCIENTECH TECHNOLOGIES PVT LTD 
 
 
 
 It is used to generate dc power to supply as input voltage/current to different testing circuits 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Mixed Signal Oscilloscope (MSO) 
 
 
 
 SCIENTECH TECHNOLOGIES PVT LTD 
 
 
 
 It is four channel MSO with different mathematical operations. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 List of Experiments: 
 
 
 
 
 
 Experimenting setup 
 
 
 
 Labs on. 
 
 
 
 Description 
 
 
 
 Snapshot 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Electronic Circuits 
 
 
 
 BJT, PN diode, Zener diode, MOSFET, JFET,  LED, Rectifier, Clipper, Clamper etc. 
 
 
 
 The lab work is based on different fundamentals electronics components. The snapshot is a circuit of LED (reverse and forward bias). 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Digital Logic Design Lab 
 
 
 
 AND, OR, NOT, XOR, XNOR, NAND, NOR, K-map, Adder, Substractor, Multiplier, Encoder, Decoder etc. 
 
 
 
 The experiments on Digital logic design are to be done depending on different logic gates ICs.  The snapshot is a circuit of AND, OR ICs. 
 
 
 
 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Analog Electronics  Lab 
 
 
 
 Inverting Op-amp, Non-Inverting Op-amp, Instrumentation Amp, Filters, Precision Ckt, PLL, Timer etc. 
 
 
 
 The experiments on Analog Electronics are to be done depending on amplifier/ attenuators ICs. The snapshot is a circuit of Op-amp IC.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Digital Electronics  Lab 
 
 
 
 Synchronous/ Asynchronous Counter, Up/Down Counter, Ripple Carry adder, JK  FF, D-FF, T-FF, Master Slave, Timer etc. 
 
 
 
 The experiments on Digital Electronics are to be done digital logic ICs. The snapshot is a circuit of RS flip-flop.   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 IT Workshop-I (Hardware Module) 
 
 
 
 Project based work generally group wise with different hardware kit. 
 
 
 
 This is project-based work for making simple ideas to work or Arduino Uno based in hardware level. The snapshot is a circuit of piano using 555 Timer. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 IT Workshop-II (Hardware Module) 
 
 
 
 Project based work generally group wise with different hardware kit. 
 
 
 
 The lab work is project based on Arduino Uno or Raspberry Pi software for IT Workshop II.  The snapshot is a circuit of Arduino Uno based firing fighting bot. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Other equipment/kit list: 
 
 
 
 
 
   
 
   
 
 
 
 List of  equipment/kit 
 
 
 
 Snapshot 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 1 
 
 
 
 Other small equipment consist of Digital IC tester, Digital Multi-meter, Bead board.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 2 
 
 
 
 It consists of   Integrated Circuit, transformer, Transistor, Seven Segment Display, Regulator, Capacitor, LED, Resistor, Diode, 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 APPLICATIONS 

This laboratory is to impart the basic knowledge of different electronic components, its characteristics and devices, to gain practical knowledge in the area of electronic components and their applications. Electronics is all about electrical components and the circuits consisting of those components. Common components are resistors, capacitors, inductors, transistors, and integrated circuits. The components are interconnecting with conductors, either physical wires or printed circuits.  The components make up linear analog amplifiers, oscillators, and filters as examples.  Students will also learn to practical, circuit design and contributes significantly to provide them with engineering skills. The Lab is well equipped with both hardware and software facilities required by the students to perform the necessary experiments designed for this lab. Experiments are designed in such a way that the students become well aware of the concepts they learn in the theory sessions.
In the Electrical Circuit Lab students can create their own electrical circuits and do measurements on it. In the circuits students can use resistors, light bulbs, switches, capacitors and coils. The circuits can be powered by a AC/DC power supply or batteries. There is an ammeter, voltmeter, wattmeter and an ohmmeter. There is also a version of the Electrical Circuit Lab in which data can be collected. Students can analyse the collected data by creating graphs of the data and use the graphs in the conclusion tool.

title: Intelligent Autonomous Systems Lab
content:
Location:  Room No. 317, Second Floor, Academic Building 
 
 About lab: 
 
 This laboratory is carefully equipped to serve the national priorities and respond to the growing expectations of the society concerning the applications of autonomous agents and Intelligent systems in education, cultural and entertainment industry, assistive technologies, healthcare, with a particular focus on the growing needs related to assistance (children, disabled and elderly people), security and control, training and supervision, and customer-service. Situated in the hearth of India, the laboratory will communicate with the rest of the scientific community. The laboratory will attract students, researchers, collaborations, and investments, representing a profitable resource for various structures, people, and interests, inside and outside our campus, becoming an example and a beacon for the whole region. 
 
 The Lab is devoted to share and provide the skills and knowledge to creatively implement a broad spectrum of AI and Robotics research methods and applications, including: Human-Robot Interactions; Multi-robot collaboration, Social and Cognitive Systems; Autonomous Systems; Robotics in Educations; Behaviors Analysis; etc. 
 
 Facilities: 
 
 The skills available at the IAS-Lab cover various methodological and algorithmic aspects of intelligent robotics and computer vision applied to robotics. These include: calibration, navigation, 3D reconstruction of environments and the interpretation of sensorial data of autonomous robots such as mobile platforms, industrial manipulators, humanoid robots and drones. 
 
 The fields of application range from service robotics to industrial robotics and from neurorobotics to agricultural robotics. 
 
 Purpose: 
 
 Demonstrate familiarity with current robot control architectures by ability to choose the most appropriate method for a given robot task, to specify the components and interactions involved, and to design and programme an algorithm that solves the task. 
 
 Identify and describe limitations of each architecture, particularly when applied to real robots interacting with the real world, rather than simulations. 
 
 Explain in detail the implementation and evaluation of a robot performing different autonomous tasks. 
 
 Areas of focus: 
 
 Robot motion planning: methods for planning robot motion to efficiently complete complex tasks. 
 
 Learning planning policies: learning robot behaviors and policies through repeated task executions.   
 
 Planning under uncertainty: planning and sensing for operation in unknown environments. 
 
 Persistent monitoring and scene reconstruction: monitoring and building real-time maps for complex 3D environments. 
 
 Future transportation systems: coordinating and dispatching vehicles for ride-sharing and urban transportation. 
 
 Active preference learning: coordinating robots to work with humans in task specification and collaborative assembly. 
 
 Distributed and submodular optimization: collective decision making strategies for objectives that exhibit diminishing returns. 
 
 Autonomous driving:  Generating safe and efficient motion in real-world environments. 
 
 Other areas of interest include dynamic vehicle routing, informative path planning, task allocation, formation control, consensus/rendezvous and ocean sampling.

title: Internet of Things and Sensor
content:
IoTs lab is at the forefront of developing both hardware including novel sensors, communication platform and analytics for Internet of Things (IoT) and smart environments. 
 
 The IoTs lab provides necessary software and equipment to develop and characterization of novel smart sensors for IoT applications. Along with these the lab is equipped with various kits like Smart Healthcare, Smart Agriculture, Smart Village and City. The main research is focused on development of a wide range of wearable sensors for pervasive monitoring, self-powered sensor nodes through energy harvesting and implementation of IoT based solution in in the area of healthcare, agriculture, and environment through the introduction of novel sensing platforms and ICT approaches. Through fusing multiple sensing modalities, we aim to develop a smart environment, which can learn and adapt to different environments and provide proactive sensing for different applications. 
 
 
 
  Lab In-charge 
 
 Dr. Debanjan Das 
 
  Assistant Professor, ECE 
 
 E-mail: debanjan@iiitnr.edu.in 
 
 
 
 
 
   
 
 Lab Assistant 
 
 Purnendra dhruw, 
 
 E-mail: purnendra@iiitnr.edu.in 
 
 
 
   
 
 Resources 
 
 Software 
 
 
 
 COMSOL Multiphysics 
 
 
 
 COMSOL Multiphysics is a powerful interactive environment for modeling and solving all kinds of scientific and engineering problems based on partial differential equations (PDEs). With this software students can easily model the sensors and devices using multiphysics models that solve coupled physics phenomena—and do simultaneously. 
 
    
 
 
 
 MATLAB ThingSpeak 
 
 
 
 ThingSpeak is an IoT analytics platform service that allows you to aggregate, visualize, and analyze live data streams in the cloud. ThingSpeak enables to prototype and build IoT systems e.g. one can send data to ThingSpeak from sensor devices, create instant visualizations of live data, and send alerts using web services. Inside ThingSpeak platform, MATLAB data analytics code can be written and executed to perform preprocessing, visualizations, and analyses. 
 
 
 
 
 
 LabVIEW 
 
 
 
 LabVIEW is an integrated development environment designed specifically for building measurement and control systems.   
 
 
 
 
 
 NetSim 
 
 
 
 NetSim simulating platform is used for modeling and analysis of IoT-network architecture. 
 
 
 
 B. Characterization and Testing 
 
 
 
 Impedance Analyzer 
 
 
 
 This laboratory houses Wayne Kerr Electronics 6500B series impedance analyzers, fully computer-controlled, for performing frequency-dependent impedance/dielectric spectroscopy studies for electrical characterization of device, materials, etc. 
 
 
 
 
 
 NI-Data Acquisition (DAQ) System 
 
 
 
 NI-Data acquisition (DAQ) is the process of measuring an electrical or physical phenomenon such as voltage, current, temperature, resistance with a computer. A DAQ system consists of sensors, DAQ measurement hardware, and a computer with programmable software. 
 
 
 
 
 
 Digital OSCILLOSCOPE 
 
 IoTs lab is equipped with SIGLENT SDS1204CFL Digital Oscilloscope, which is a compact desktop two -channel  digital oscilloscope with a 200MHz bandwidth 
 
 
 
 
 Function/  Waveform generator 
 
 
 
 The lab facilitates arbitrary function generators and power supply for initial testing for developed sensors and circuits. 
 
 
 
 
 
 DC Power Supply 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 pH/conductivity/ion analyzer 
 
 
 
 The conductivity measurement involves AC current measurement through a column of solution under an external reference AC voltage source. When passed through a known series resistance, the current furnishes voltage output which is a measure of conductance. pH determination involves determination of millivolts developed across a pair of electrodes (glass and calomel) when dipped into a solution. 
 
 
 
 
 
 LCD Digital Microscope 
 
 
 
 It allows you to view the specimen and get a much more accurate view of its surface. It can efficiently handle the viewing of transparent and solid specimens with a magnification of between ten and forty times, making the tiniest details of a specimen’s surface clearly visible for analyzing. With fully achromatic lens objectives, a mechanical stage, 3.5” full color TFT screen and 5MP CMOS sensor, the LCD Digital Microscope allows for high-resolution imaging of specimen. 
 
 
 
 C. Process and fabrication 
 
 At present IoTs lab facilitates following equipment for polymer (mainly PDMS), paper-electrode and textile based sensors and devices.  
 
 
 
 Spin Coater 
 
 
 
 Spin Coating is a standard laboratory procedure which involves depositing thin films of materials, often polymers, uniformly on flat substrates by employing the concept of centrifugal force. In short, an excess amount of a solution is placed on the substrate, which is then rotated at high speed in order to spread the fluid by centrifugal force. 
 
 
 
 
 
 Vacuum Pump 
 
 
 
 Rocker series piston-powered, oil-free vacuum pump is used for creating vacuum for different applications. 
 
 
 
 
 
 Lab Heating Plate 
 
 
 
 This is a PID controlled heating plates where two different values of temperature can be maintained simultaneously. 
 
 
 
 D. Prototyping Facility 
 
 3D Printer         
 3D printers, also known as 3D rapid-prototypers, are capable of taking digital geometry in the form of a stereolithographic file (.stl) and producing small models. One 3D printer with build volume of 300x300x350 mm3 and material support of PLA, ABS, Nylon, Flex is available for the students. 
 
 
                                                               
 
 
   
 
 IoT based sensing platform 
 
 This lab is equipped with development kits like 
 
 Raspberry Pi 3 Boards 
 
 Arduino UNO Boards 
 
 Quad-core ARM Cortex-A53 based IOT trainer kit 
 
 GSM and GPS Modules 
 
 Bluetooth, and WiFi modules 
 
 NI Biosensor Kit

title: Language Lab
content:
The blended methodology used at the Language Laboratory of IIIT-NR caters to the language needs of the students by providing them suitable environment for self-paced effective language learning. The primary objective of the lab is to enhance language learning skills of the students based on their individual requirements. 
 
 The lab is well-equipped with Digital Language Learning Software platform that aims in facilitating efficient teacher- student interaction to correct phonetic accents and dialects. It also aims at carrying out the following activities: 
 
 Improves various aspects of pronunciation like rate of speech, intonation and voice modulation 
 
  Enhances ability to comprehend native accents and idioms 
 
 Improves grammatical and vocabulary skills

 Dr. Sresha Yadav 
 
 sresha@iiitnr.edu.in

title: Networks Lab
content:
Laboratory Name and Location: Network Lab, Room No. 132, Ground Floor, Academic Building 
 
 About Lab: 
 
 The Networks Lab with state-of-the-art facility established to provide hands-on learning and experimentation in network technologies. It is equipped with cutting-edge hardware and software resources, enabling students and researchers to explore various aspects of networking, from basic principles to advanced concepts. The Network Lab is a unified setup that supports a wide range of educational and research activities, enabling UG, PG and Ph. D students to gain practical skills in network setup, management, and security. The facility also serves as a platform for exploring emerging technologies such as wired-wireless networking, advanced computer network, network security, and Software-Defined Networking (SDN). Through interactive experiments and hands-on projects, undergraduates, post-graduates, research scholars, and interns’ students to develop a deeper understanding of network infrastructures, protocols, and the underlying technologies that drive modern communication systems. 
 
 Objective: 
 
 1. Provide Hands-On Experience: 
 
 Equip students and researchers with practical skills in configuring, managing, and troubleshooting network systems through hands-on experiments and projects. 
 
 2. Enhance Understanding of Networking Concepts: 
 
 Facilitate a deeper understanding of both foundational and advanced networking concepts, including network architecture, protocols, routing, switching, and network services. 
 
 3. Promote Research and Innovation: 
 
 Support innovative research in emerging areas of networking, such as Software-Defined Networking (SDN), Network security etc. 
 
 4. Develop Cybersecurity Skills: 
 
 Train students in network security practices, including the use of tools and techniques for monitoring network traffic, detecting vulnerabilities, and implementing security measures to protect data and resources. 
 
 5. Support Software-Defined Networking (SDN) and Programmable Networks: 
 
 Provide a platform for exploring and experimenting with SDN and programmable network technologies, enabling students to understand and develop solutions for modern, flexible, and efficient network infrastructures 
 
 6. Facilitate Interdisciplinary Learning: 
 
 Support interdisciplinary learning by integrating networking principles with other fields such as computer science, cybersecurity, data science, and blockchain, thus broadening the scope and application of networking knowledge. 
 
 Outcomes: 
 
 Hands-on lab sessions for B.Tech, M.Tech, MS, and Ph. D students, enhancing their practical understanding of technologies. 
 
 Students undertake mini, minor, and major projects as part of their curriculum, using the lab to design, implement, and test their solutions. 
 
 PhD, M Tech, interns students utilized the lab for their projects and research, exploring and developing innovative solutions. 
 
 Producing high-quality research, resulting in reputed journal articles and conference papers. 
 
 The lab hosts workshops, Faculty Development Programs (FDPs), and hands-on training sessions for in-house as well as external students, faculties, and industry professionals.

title: PCB Design Lab
content:
This lab features equipment and machines for the production of multi-layer Printed circuit boards (PCBs). 
 
   
 
 Lab Facilities: 
 
 Printed circuit boards are electronic circuits created for mounting electronic components on a nonconductive board, and creating conductive connections between them. They are created by using both additive and subtractive methods. The most common type of printed circuit board is double-sided, although single-sided and multi-layered versions exist as well. 
 
 Single sided PCB: Conductors on only one side of a dielectric base. 
 
   Double sided PCB: Conductors on both sides of a dielectric base, usually interconnected (the two layers) with plated-through-holes (PTHs).

title: Winter School on IoT and ML
content:
About the Course 
 
 This Winter School teaches you how to build a competent job-ready profile to start a career in the fields of IoT and Machine Learning. It doesn't matter if you don't have a background in Programming or Hardware Development. Everything you need to know taught in this program step-by-step starting from scratch with hands on practice on customized IoT Kits and Machine Learning Models. 
 
 Motivation 
 
 Everyone today is aware of the big boom of Internet of Things (IoT) and Machine Learning (ML) in the market. Almost all domains like healthcare, entertainment, manufacturing, agriculture, academics and defense require Machine Learning and IoT in some way or the other. This 5-days winter school will provide the basics and the broad picture regarding IoT and ML. Importantly participants will be provided with one-on-one mentoring, extensive doubt clearing sessions and support from experts in IIIT Naya Raipur. 
 
   Who can attend: 
 
 The course is open for ITI, Diploma, BSc, MSc, BCA, MCA, BTech, MTech Students, Faculties and Working Professionals from MSME, Industries interested in the fields of IoT and Machine Learning 
 
 Seats are limited and confirmed on first come first basis. 
 
 Benefits 
 
 
 Trending in Job Market: These are skills with high demand and scarce supply in the market right now. 
 
 Winter School Certificate from IIIT Naya Raipur: No Pre-Requisites: Starts from scratch - Teaches Arduino, RPI, python programming and all required 
 
 Activity Based Learning: Case study and Project-driven learning methodology. 
 
 Extensive hands-on experience both in software and hardware. 
 
 Expert instructors from IIIT

title: Robotics & AI
content:
Motivation 
 
 The Robotics and Autonomous systems is an interdisciplinary field which interacts and combines with various engineering branches of electrical, electronics, mechanical and computer science/information technology. Fueled by recent advances in computer vision, sensing, digitization, fabrication and understanding the dynamics of physical systems, may open for new technological advances in Robotics. Robotics may find applications range from machine tools, autonomous vehicles, medical, process industry for automation, military. Some applications are already mature and new ones emerge every day. Therefore, Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (AI) have gained a rapidly expanding foothold in the workplace, faster than many organizations ever. This is predicted to be one of the most wanted skills in the near-term future!. Therefore learning robotics fundamentals will be a very handy thing. 
 
 Objectives 
 
 This course is designed to provide an exposure to the fundamentals of Robotics and Artificial Intelligence. Participants will learn kinematics and dynamics of industrial manipulators, kinematics of mobile robots, trajectory planning, path planning and control and how to embed intelligence in robotic tasks. Therefore, the course provides basic understanding of mathematical methods for modeling and control of robot manipulators. Machine learning and artificial intelligence is an integral part in understanding of robotics, will be discussed. Hands-on training and practice sessions will help participants gain confidence on robotic concepts, their simulation and implementation including sessions on intelligent agents. A practical session of MATLAB based integration of hardware and software is also included for real world applications. Each day will have a focused theme on a particular area. A practical session on integration of hardware with software is also included for real world applications. The course will be useful for students, faculty of engineering and sciences and industry person who are interested in the learning robotics and intelligent systems 
 
 Who can attend: 
 
 Programme is open to following audiences, who are working in the field of Electrical, Electronics & Communication, Instrumentation and Control, Mechatronics and Robotics. 
 
 B.Tech/ M.Tech/ Ph.D students 
 
 Faculty members of engineering colleges 
 
 Practicing Robotics Engineers and Industry professionals 
 
 Seats are limited and confirmed on first come first basis. 
 
 Course Content 
 
 
 
 Speakers 
 
 Dr. Debanjan Das, Assistant Professor, Dept. of ECE, IIIT Naya Raipur 
 
 Dr. Souvick Chatterjee, MathWorks 
 
 Registration

title: Evolution of Modern Wireless & Cognitive Radio Networks: 5G Perspectives
content:
Last date of registration: 20th January 2020. 
 
 Motivation: 
 
 With the rapid proliferation of smart technologies in our day-to-day lives, an ever-growing demand of high data rates and enhanced quality has been imposed on the existing cellular networks. The future 5G wireless communication promises to meet these demands by guaranteeing data rates of the order of Gbps with an extremely low latency and a significant enhancement in the users’ Quality of Experience (QoE). Thus, researchers across both industry and academia are focusing heavily on development of cutting-edge technologies which can act as suitable contenders to meet the throughput and quality demands of 5G networks. 
 
   Objectives 
 
 This course intends to provide a comprehensive and in-depth treatment of various concepts of the widely popular cognitive radio networks (CRNs) and its relevance in the context of the upcoming 5G wireless systems along with a practical hands-on simulation experience. The theory covered during the short course will help lay down the foundation for understanding advanced concepts and research problems in the area of CRNs. Every theoretical session shall be followed by a hands-on session on USRP/GNU Radio where several mini projects shall be demonstrated to the participants in order to give a practical flavour of various components of 5G wireless communication and CRNs. Finally, the short course shall also discuss some of the interesting open research problems related to 5G in CRNs. 
 
 Target Audience: 
 
   Practising Wireless   Engineers and Industry professionals 
 
 B.Tech/ M.Tech/ Ph.D students 
 
   Faculty members of engineering colleges

title: Workshop on Blockchain Technology
content:
Click here for Online Registration 
 
 About the Workshop 
 
 Blockchain Technology is an emerging technology suitable for decentralised and transactional sharing of data across a large network of untrusted participants. It is an innovative distributed ledger technology where verification data for the transactional records is cryptographically secured against tampering and stored in blocks. Each block contains details of transactions, hash of the previous block, timestamp etc. Blocks are linked with each other, leading to a Block Chain and this is stored at every node in the network. This enables transparency and gives a unique solution to the problem of trust in the digital world, leading towards decentralised trust. It has good potential to transform the logging and management of transactions in different application areas. This technology is successfully used in Bitcoin virtual currency and it also has good potential in non-financial sectors such as Governance, healthcare, cyber security, automobiles, media, travel, hospitality, energy, smart cities, time stamping, logging of important events in a system, recording transactions, etc. 
 
 This workshop is intended to provide opportunities for faculty, researchers and students to upgrade their knowledge in the area of Blockchain Technologies. Considering the growing importance of Blockchain Technologies and its potential in different sectors, this three day workshop is planned and sessions would be delivered by experts from academia and industry. 
 
 Workshop Objectives 
 
 To disseminate the knowledge of Blockchain technology among students, researchers and academicians. 
 
 To discuss the current status of the Blockchain development and research work in India and across the world. 
 
 Workshop Topics 
 
 Introduction to Blockchain Technology 
 
 Evolution of Block Chain Technology 
 
 Understanding Block Chain Technology and Cryptography Behind it 
 
 Blockchain Architecture and Components 
 
 Bitcoin 
 
 Permissions & Permission-less Block Chains 
 
 Consensus Protocols 
 
 Smart Contracts 
 
 Block Chain Technology Standards 
 
 Block Chain Technology Applications & Global Scenario 
 
 Demonstration of Block Chain Application 
 
 The future of Block Chain Technology 
 
 Who should Attend: Members from Industry, Government Organisations, Faculty of Engineering Colleges, Researches and PG/UG students. 
 
 Certificate: A Certificate of participation will be awarded to each participant on conclusion of the programme. 
 
 Accommodation: Free accommodation will be provided in the campus guest house and hostels on sharing basis.

title: Sensor Networks, Internet of Things (IoT) and Internet of Everything
content:
Click here for Online Registration 
 
 Last date of registration: 9th Dec 2019. 
 
 Motivation: 
 
 Broadly, the concept of wireless  sensor network  (WSN) is a type of ad-hoc network consisting of several nodes followed by one or more gateway nodes (GN) or Base Station (BS). All the sensor nodes are autonomously or distributed way to monitor/sensing physical or environmental conditions. After sensing the data, sensor nodes send it to the user wither through gateway or Base Station. It is challenging in terms of energy efficient also now a days that the WSN may be heterogeneous in nature. In this model, the concept of IoT can be applied also for the entire sensor node where all the nodes are connected to Internet for transmitting information. Hence, security and privacy is also one of the important challenging issues for message confidentiality.  This is to enhance/upgrade the knowledge of the participants in the field of WSN and IoT with security concerns. It will emphasize the current research challenges of the same domain. 
 
 Objectives:  
 
 In modern life, WSN and IoT plays an important role for their several applications such as road safety, smart city, street light etc. In order to make all the application very effortlessly, it is indeed required the knowledge of all these technology. The main objective of the workshop is 
 
 To explore the knowledge of basic architecture of Sensor in details and how these sensor node forms a network model to use in several applications. 
 
 How the internet of things (IoT) can be applied in sensor network model for achieving better performance. 
 
 To explore the knowledge of different kinds of WSN model such as cluster free/cluster based, mobile base station based, homogeneous, heterogeneous etc. 
 
 One of the parts of this model is security and privacy, which can be also considered as discussion in this workshop.   
 
 To promote interactions and collaborations among all the participants and to exchange and stimulate ideas of Sensor Networks, IoT and Internet of Everything. 
 
 Target Audience: 
 
 Engineers and Industry professionals 
 
 B. Tech/ M. Tech/ Ph. D students 
 
 Faculty members of Engineering Institutes

title: FDP on Cyber security And Blockchain Technologies
content:
Objective of the course: 
 
 Modern information and system require high security protection on electronic data over insecure communication (Internet). Cyber security or Information Technology Security (ITC) is a field involving the protection of computer systems and the prevention of unauthorized access too.  The primary objective of this course is to deliver on modern cryptographic techniques as well as hardware security concept with cybercrime issues to understand how to design secure systems for computer network model.  This course moreover focuses on current emerging technology on blockchain along with future research direction in the domain of network security. 
 
 Last date of registration is 25 th November 2019 
 
 Course Content: 
 
 Foundations of Cryptography 
 
 Block-Chain Technologies 
 
 Cloud Security 
 
 Hardware Security 
 
 Modern Cryptography 
 
 Research Directions in Cyber Security and Block-Chain Technologies 
 
 Cybercrimes issues

 Digital Forensics

title: Deep Learning & Applications in  Computer Vision
content:
Deep Learning & Applications in Computer Vision 
 
 & 
 
 Natural Language Processing  (20 - 24 January 2020) 
 
 
 Objectives of the course 
 
 This course is designed to provide the participants with an exposure to the fundamentals of Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and Deep Learning (DL). Participants will learn the basics of Deep Learning methods ranging from Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), Long Short – Term Memory (LSTM), Autoencoders and Variational Autoencoders (VAEs). These techniques can be applied to solve real-world research or industry problems. The hands-on training and practice sessions will ensure that every participant gains confidence in working with deep learning concepts by creating their own DL models. The course will be useful for any researcher who is willing to apply the Deep Learning methods in their field of application. Especially those faculty members who are venturing into Deep Learning research shall learn the applications in areas like image processing, computer vision and Natural Language Processing. 
 
 About IIIT-Naya Raipur 
 
 Dr. Shyama Prasad Mukherjee IIIT Naya Raipur (IIITNR), a joint initiative of Chhattisgarh Government and National Thermal Power Corporation (NTPC) commits to pursuing excellence in higher education, research and development in ICT. To empower this vision, IIITNR provides state-of-the-art research and education facilities. IIITNR offers UG programs in CSE, ECE, DSAI (Data Science & Artificial Intelligence) stream along with PG programs in CSE and ECE as well as a PhD program in all disciplines. The dynamic structure of the curriculum orchestrates with the changing needs of industry and academia.

title: IT Infrastructure
content:
IIIT-NR has truly world class, campus-wide state of the art information technology tools that are designed to meet the computing and communication needs of the institute, which has a fast, reliable and rugged computer network of more than 700 Nodes. In order to facilitate proper teaching aid, all classrooms here are equipped with projectors and audio systems and has two smart classrooms. All the hostels, cafeteria, academic block, guest house, multipurpose hall and residences, are connected through redundant 10 gigabit fiber backbone network. All the blocks are connected through layer 2 & 3 switches to provide 1Gbps connectivity at the user end. Wireless network with 84 indoor access points  to give total campus Wi-Fi coverage. All traffic for internet both inbound and outbound passes though 1+1 redundant UTM. 
 
 Internet Access : Through a 500 Mbps Internet leased line ( In ring Arctiticture), 1Gbps ( In ring Arctiticture) and  100 Mbps from NKN   with an  active–active configuration.   Internet connection range is provided through both LAN and Wi-Fi in all blocks inclusive of residences, academic, dinning and hostels. 
 
                                      Our team 
 
 
 
 ABIR BHATTACHARYA 
 
 
 SENIOR NETWORK ENGINEER 
 Where To Find:- 131C 
 Email:- abir@iiitnr.edu.in   
 Phone:771-2474010 
 
 
 
 
 
 PARVINDER SINGH 
 
 
 NETWORK ENGINEER 
 Where to find:- 131E 
 Email: -parvinder@iiitnr.edu.in   
 Phone:771-2474034 
 
 
 
 
 
 LALIT MARKANDEY 
 
 
 LABORATORY ASSISTANT 
 Email:- lalit@iiitnr.edu.in   
 
 
 
 Data Centre : IIIT-NR has a full-fledged data center of its own. It hosts 5 half blade form factor Inten XEON servers in a 16 slot chassis and has network unified storage of 35 TB. The datacentre is powered by redundant 100KVA UPS with N+1 backup.  All servers are connected to Internet by public IPs. 
 
 Virtual machines on cloud: -   The Institute has VMware Venter cluster perpetual licenses. VM cluster is formed with 1 Physical server failover configuration with total computation power of 80 CPU cores and 1TB of RAM.  All logical servers are created on top VM hypervisor which is managed by Venter server, this setup is a private cloud setup for IIIT-NR usage. Date of all virtual machines are kept at SAN and are backed up weekly using VMware EMC backup system.Institute also uses open source  ProxMox Hypervisor in cluster for one Xeon server dedicated for under grad students project. 
 
 Infrastructure Servers: - IT infrastructure uses servers for providing various important services to the institute. The servers are primarily all open source Linux based system. Severs like webserver, mail server, DHCP server, Active directory and internal DNS server. Public DNS server, helpdesk server, Library server, Moodle server, Oracle database server, and many more license server used for infrastructure of the institute. All the servers are created on VM cluster as mentioned above. 
 
 Compute Server: - IT Department issues server infrastructure to students, faculty members to cater their computational needs on requirement basis. This is also issued from the VM cluster as mentioned above. Currently 2 computational servers have been issued to students and faculty to carry our research and project work. Apart for that One server with open source ProxMox Hypervisor in  dedicated for Under grad students project. 
 
 IP Telephony : Our entire Campus is equipped with IP telephony. It has 75 IP phones.   
 
 Video Conferencing : Polycom Real presence HD   VC System is available with multi party conference facility.  
 
 VPN : It can be used to access all IIIT-NR IT resources from outside campus using any Internet connection. 
 
 Software : Institute holds licenses on various software like Cisco WebEx,  VMware, Microsoft Office, Synopsys , Cadence , Ansys, Matlab , Netsim, Oracle, Wordsworth, CST etc. These are used by students, staff and faculty members. LabView,  Simulink, Orcad 
 
 
 
 
 
   
   
   
 
 Operating Systems and Services:-  The institute mainly focuses on usage of free operating system like Ubuntu etc. But keeping in view of long term requirements, the institute also has licenses for Microsoft Windows MSDN dreams park account to upgrade/install their Microsoft products. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Desktop clients:-   The institute has 500desktops, 40 workstations at various labs and offices, 45 laptops and 70 printers and scanners. For labs institute has one server, 35 TB  Network unified storage dedicated for high end computing 
 
 
 
 Eclassroom :-   Institute has  two 120 sitters E-Classrooms. Equipped for two way (remote and IIIT-NR) interactive lectures. In this setup  16 simultaneous classrooms can be connected. Live streaming and recoding of lectures are being done using the Eclassroom Facility.

title: Library At A Glance
content:
CLICK HERE TO ACCESS ONLINE LIBRARY MANAGEMENT SYSTEM 
 
 Institute library is part of the administrative building spanned over 2 floors acquiring plinth area of approximately 800 sq. meters with central air-conditioning system and open wi-fi. 
 
 Its primary aim is to: 
 
 Support the institute’s mission “To Create an Institute of Excellence in IT Education and Research for Meeting the Emerging and Challenging Needs of the Society.” 
 
 The mission and vision of Information Center is very closely based on the premises of Five Laws of Library Science propounded by renowned mathematician and father of library sciences Dr SR Ranganathan. These are: 
 
 Books/Information is for use. 
 
 Every reader his book/ information. 
 
 Every book/ information its reader. 
 
 Save the time of the reader. 
 
 Library is a growing organism. 
 
 Collection Development :  We religiously follow the second and the third law when it comes to collection development. We go a step ahead when it comes to making available resources whether print or electronic by involving all the stake holders e.g, faculty members, students and researchers. It complements and supplements the scholarly and informational needs of faculty, students and researchers. Right from the day of inception it is aggressively building on its collection in varieties of forms like printed text books, reference books , magazines, newspapers and e resources like IEEE xplore, E-book collection of McGraw Hills etc. Digital Library section of the Central Library has developed an institutional digital repository using open source software DSpace, which preserve the institute's intellectual output for the posterity. Library has a separate section for books on history/flora & fauna of Chhattisgarh.

title: Fenestra --Institute Magazine
content:
It gives us immense joy and satisfaction to re-introduce our very own institute magazine - ‘ Fenestra ’. The magazine encompasses the above thought of all young minds. Just as the ‘Asuras’ and ‘Devas’ churned out ‘Amrit’ from the ocean, we have tried to churn out creativity from the technical minds of this institute. An institute narrates its story through the pages of its magazine. Opinions are like windows. When we allow light to come in, it results in a work like Fenestra. As ‘Fenestra’ means window, it acts as a window for all occasions, activities, triumphs and celebrations in the institute. From events to achievements in every field, it renders a full-fledged idea of IIIT-NR and its culture. Hope you give it a read!. Any complaints, queries, submissions, criticisms and suggestions regarding the magazine to the editorial team are invited. 
 
 The editorial team can be contacted through Email at : fenestra@iiitnr.edu.in

title: IIIT-NR CONNECT MOBILE APPLICATION
content:
About The App:- 
 
 IIIT Connect (Version 2.0) launched on 11th August 2016 by Rohit Kumar Singh and Arijit Saha 
 
 Currently Only Available on Android Devices. 
 
 
 Specifications of the App:- 
 
 1. Book Bazzar:-It is the online e-commerce app based portal where old engineering books are sold to the new students at a rate which is decided very low. 
 
 2. Notifications:-You will get immediate notifications of the upcoming events like tests, submission of assignments and different events going to happen in the campus. 
 
 3. Events/Happening:-It lists down both the Upcoming events and the Latest events in details of IIIT Naya Raipur. 
 
 4. Photo Gallery:- It makes you relive a moment by showing you the photographs of different events of IIITNR. 
 
 5. Important Documents/Notices:-It displays the important notices like Academic Calendar, Mess Menu, Examination Schedues, Time Table. 
 
 6. Student Of the Month:- The most interesting feature of IIIT Connect. Here both students and faculty members can vote their favourite student using their unique Verification Code. 
 
 7. Student Contact:- It displays the details of all students of IIITNR including their Contact Numbers, Email ID, Branch, Room No's.

title: Anti Ragging Committee
content:
In compliance with the decisions of Hon’ble Supreme Court of India and consequent Gazette notification of “University Grant Commission Regulations 2013” for curbing the Menace of Ragging in Higher Educational Institutions and also as notified by AICTE vide its notification no. 37-3/Legal/AICTE/2009 dated 25-03-2009, the competent authority has constituted following Committees as part of Anti ragging measures in IIITNR campus; 
 
 Institute Level Anti-Ragging Committee 
 
 VC & Director - Chair 
 
 Dean (Academics) - Member 
 
 Chairman (HMC)- Member 
 
 SAC President - Member 
 
 Dr. Sresha Yadav nee Ghosh - Member 
 
 Representative of parents (fresher, Senior) 
 
 Student representatives (fresher, Senior) 
 
 Registrar - Member Secretary 
 
 Anti Ragging Flying squads: 
 
 The proposed Anti-Ragging Squads shall be empowered to inspect all such places which are vulnerable to incidents of ragging and shall ensure vigil in all such locations. Hostel Level Squad shall also be empowered to inspect all places in the Hostel which are vulnerable to incidents of ragging and shall ensure vigil in all such locations. The Hostel Level Anti-Ragging Squad shall publish the contact information (including Mobile numbers) of it’s members as follows;

title: Outreach 2025
content:
OIP 2025: Selected candidate list 
 
 Outreach Internship Programme (OIP) is one of the research initiatives of IIIT Naya Raipur, to bring students from prominent institutions across the country right here to our campus. The institute has been organizing this programme since 2017. Many students who participated in the previous programme have benefited from this programme, which is evident through the papers published by them in journals and conferences of repute. With this motivation, this year also, IIIT-NR invites students from all over India to spend 6 to 8 weeks in the institute, carrying out research under the supervision of IIIT-NR faculty members and making use of state-of-art laboratory facilities at IIIT-NR. The OIP programme allows students to participate in ongoing research projects and simultaneously enables them to fulfill the summer internship requirements of their respective academic programs. Selected interns will get the chance to work diligently with motivated researchers to: 
 
 Innovate technical solutions for core research areas 
 
 Create and deliver a pioneering solution for real-life problems 
 
 Publish their work in reputed conferences and journals 
 
 Contribute towards patents filing 
 
 IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS 
 
 Mode of Carrying out the Internship: Offline only. 
 
 For whom (Eligibility): Students from other reputed institutes pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree are eligible to do an internship in IIIT-NR under the supervision of any faculty member at IIIT-NR. 
 
 The candidate must be from CSE, IT, ECE, ETE, EE, EEE, EI, Mathematics, Physics, Management, and related backgrounds. B.Tech. candidates must be completing their 6th semester (3rd year). 
 
 Duration: An intern has to work offline for 6 to 8 weeks to receive a stipend and certificate. Students may join earlier/later as per their academic calendar, but the duration should be at least 6 weeks. 
 
 .Stipend will be provided to selected students on successful internship completion and his/her faculty supervisor’s recommendation. 
 
 Selection: A committee comprising faculty members will screen and finalize the candidates for accepting as Summer Interns and attach each intern with a faculty supervisor on the explicit willingness of a faculty mentor. The 
 
 
 
 
 Committee will select the interns based on CGPA/interview/project proposal, technical accomplishments, etc. 
 
 The candidates will be informed about the acceptance of their candidature subject to fulfillment of Institute Rules. 
 
 Selected candidates will have to report at IIIT Naya Raipur on the reporting date with an Internship Letter, Application Form, etc. Due to genuine hardships, a delay of 2-3 days can be granted on prior requests. 
 
 Accommodation: Interns will be provided hostel accommodation (if required) with nominal charges. The accommodation facility is subject to availability. Other than hostel charges the intern must pay for the cost of food and access to other amenities. 
 
 Applicants must submit a report at the end of the internship tenure. 
 
 Note: Interested candidates must apply online in the prescribed format on or before 15th May 2025. No separate application through letter or email will be entertained. 
 
 TIMELINE 
 
 Tentative Schedule any changes will be notified on our website 
 
   
 
 
 
 S. 
 
 No. 
 
 
 
 Scheduled Activity 
 
 
 
 Timeline 
 
 
 
 
 1 
 
 
 
 Date of opening of online application 
 
 
 
 10th April 2025 
 
 
 
 
 2 
 
 
 
 Last date of closing online applications 
 
 
 
 15th May 2025 
 
 
 
 
 3 
 
 
 
 Date of announcement of selected applicants 
 
 
 
 19th May 2025 
 
 
 
 
 4 
 
 
 
 Internship duration 
 
 
 
 26th May 2025 to 12th July 2025 
 
 
 
 
 5 
 
 
 
 Reporting for internship 
 
 
 
 26th May 2025 
 
 
 
 
 6 
 
 
 
 Submission of report 
 
 
 
 One week before the end of the internship 
 
 
 
 
 7 
 
 
 
 Issuance of certificates 
 
 
 
 25th July 2025 
 
 
 HOW TO APPLY 
 
 During application form submission, the candidate must submit a photograph/signature in digital format and recommendation letter from his/her HoD/faculty with an institute seal. 
 
 Click here to apply Online for Outreach Internship Programme 2025 
 
 Please fill out the online form correctly. 
 
 Note: Applicants should not send filled application form hardcopy to IIIT Naya Raipur 
 
 Faculty profiles and their research areas can be found here 
 
 Faculty current research topics can be found here 
 
 
    Note: Please contact outreach@iiitnr.ac.in for any query 
 
 
 
 FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE 
 
 Internship and stay at IIIT Naya Raipur involve some expenditure to be incurred by the interns. 
 
 Summer interns may get a stipend if they work satisfactorily for 6 to 8 weeks. Interns may join earlier/later as per their academic calendar for at least 6 weeks. In other cases, no stipend will be provided; only a certificate will be issued to the intern. 
 
 Only a limited number of stipends are available for summer interns on the recommendation of their faculty supervisor. 
 
 SELECTED STUDENTS 
 
 Final Approved list of Students for Outreach Internship Programme 2025 List of students selected for OIP 2025 
 
 IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS FOR INTERNSHIP STUDENTS 
 
 Upon Arrival 
 
 Students need to report at the main gate with an Internship Letter. 
 
 They will be directed to their place of accommodation for their rooms (for candidates who have asked for accommodation). 
 
 A hard copy of the Parent institute ID card, Internship Letter, Application Form (hard copy), and the requisite documents signed by the student should be submitted to the IIIT-NR office when joining the internship. 
 
 At IIIT Naya Raipur 
 
 Students need to sign the attendance register kept in the institute’s office without fail by 10 AM and 6 PM. Failure to put attendance leads to absence from the institute. 
 
 PREVIOUS OIPs 
 
 Every year, OIP attracts a large number of applications from various higher- educational institutions across India. Out of these, selected students from different institutes get an opportunity to do an internship at IIIT-NR. Some are from the State of CG, while others are from reputed institutions like IITs, NITs, IIITs, well- known private universities, etc., all over India. IIIT-NR is proud to host, guide, and provide them exposure to research through its state-of-art laboratories, recent advanced technologies, tools, and library facilities, in addition to financial support. 
 
 The purpose of the OIP is to enable undergraduate students to carry out independent research in their field of interest. Students also get to attend special 
 
 
   lectures and workshops, making their stay a rewarding experience. In their feedback, some interns expressed that at IIIT-NR, they gained exposure to new technologies and hands-on experience with various advanced tools that they needed to be made aware of and were not available at their parent institutions.

title: STUDENTS SPEAK
content:
Leaving the comfort of home and stepping into college for further studies can be an amazing experience if you are wishing to pursue your engineering studies from IIIT Naya Raipur. With      exciting fests, events, workshops, along with supportive seniors and freindly professors, IIIT-NR campus is a fantastic place to spend four years of your life…….. Prachi Priyam, B.Tech  (CSE) Student 
  Focussing on academic front, programs like SYMPOSIUM 2k16 on Bussiness Intelligence and Big Data Analytics gave the students a wide exposure to Big Data World. DARPAN 2k16, a self-introspection workshop has taken an initiative in inculcating self-confidence, self-awareness and solid foundations of knowledge. These programs have been successful  in reflecting innovation and creativity among students…… Bahroz Hassan, B.Tech. (ECE) Student 
 
 It gives me immense satisfaction to have completed an exciting first year at IIIT-NR. The journey has been a remarkable one. I made new friends at IIIT-NR. Also we (Our Director, Faculty and Students) regard ourselves as a member of a family- “The IIIT-NR Family”. The Professors here are highly knowledgeable and experienced in their respective fields. IIIT-NR gives a great opportunity for students to broaden their knowledge beyond their field of studies within a multicultural environment.  The institute has hi-tech and robust infrastructure and associated facilities like Hi-Speed Internet Access, Library and others. It is a moment of extreme happiness and immense responsibility to maintain the reputation of this epic family of which I am proud to be a part of……………… Siddhant Dash, B.Tech. (ECE) Student 
 
   My ambition is not only to complete my degree successfully, but to make the best out of the time spent studying. The IIIT-NR teaching staff are very supportive and amongst the best in their genre. You can count on them to inspire you to bring out the best in yourself and even the most difficult and intricate topics will be taught with the utmost professionalism. IIIT-NR is equipped with smart classrooms, fully-furnished labs and library. The Institute offers an ideal environment for education and research. IIIT-NR has been truly a world class institute.……. Prashant Dhillon, B.Tech. (ECE) Student 
  If you are looking for world class education in the field of Information Technology, you are at the right place. At IIIT-NR, a student flourishes personally as well as professionally. The institute is currently focussing on quality education to each student so that one can enhance themselves not only as an engineer, but also as an entrepreneur. IIIT-NR harbours a healthy campus culture with lively environment. Here, the Hostel life is the best part of students’ life which certainly helps in coming out as a strong and independent person……. Pooja Jaiswal, B.Tech. (CSE) Student 
                                                                                                            Since the first moment I came to IIIT-NR, I was met with nothing but energy and enthusiasm.  The faculty and staff at this institution are top rate.  They all want their students to do well.  This institution has so many opportunities for the students to get the help they need and they deserve. It is a privilege to be a part of this institution…………… Gaurav Gupta, B.Tech. (ECE) Student 
  It is a pleasing experience to be part of IIIT-NR, where I am able to nurture my dreams and turn them into reality. It provides a platform where students can learn, experience and sharpen their skills…….. Ajeet Singh, B.Tech. (ECE) Student 
  I am a B.Tech. CSE student of IIIT-NR. Till now, I have cherished every moment of my stay in the campus. IIIT-NR is a spellbound mixture of fun and studies. Cirriculum of the university is very good and can be compared with any of the other premier institutes…… Aman Varma, B.Tech. (CSE) Student 
  IIIT-NR, a place where knowledge blooms and education blossoms. Benevolent professors and ever ready staff make the scope for better education. An ideal institute for the engineering aspirants who seek to pursue their career in technology. Campus life of IIIT-NR brings out creativity and innovation among students encouraging them to build their own way to success."Seek and ye shall find"…….. Ananya Srivastava, B.Tech. (ECE) Student 
  The deep faith in the dictum "One's own reaction to, observations about and understanding of something are more important than someone else's opinion about it", became the cornerstone of IIIT-NR, and this cherished conviction inspired and guided me to spread my wings to fly. IIIT-NR educates students in a creative way and encourages us to think out of the box. However, the institute not only helps me to excel in the academic field, but also teaches me a lot about different cultures and how to appreciate them. The good learning environment, excellent teachers, labs, library and infrastructure at IIIT-NR, helps a student to acquire knowledge vividly…. Aditi, B.Tech. (ECE) Student   
  IIIT-NR has provided a great platform for all the students to explore themselves –personally and professionally. The environment is friendly and inviting, the faculty and supporting staff would do anything for you to be the best that you can be while here, and you learn more than just what is being taught in the classroom. IIIT-NR is where people are motivated to acquire knowledge and engage the world around them . …. Hansa Meghwani, B.Tech. (CSE) Student 
  IIIT-NR's multicultural campus helps in creating an environment of knowledge and skill sharing. The vibrant nature of the campus has brought out the creativity, innovation and mutual understanding among the students …. Twinkle Singh, B.Tech. (ECE) Student 
  
  The peaceful atmosphere of the campus is exemplary for the academic and the residential life. With the faculty members also staying in the residential campus, the students are always in touch with them and there is always a presence of educational environment in the campus. The Infrastructure of the campus gives special impetus to the cultural, athletic and educational activities, the campus of IIIT-NR just feels like a different learning medium, thus providing umpteen opportunities for the growth and development of the student in various disciplines. The best way to get a notion of the campus is to join the IIIT-NR family and create your own experience…. Yash Aavart Pathak, B.Tech. (ECE) Student

title: Vocational Training Programme

title: Industry Academia Collaboration cum Advisory Committee
content:
Mr. Sujit Baksi ( President, Corporate Affairs and Head India Business, Tech Mahindra ) 
 
 
 
 Sujit Baksi is an accomplished business leader with over 30 years of experience in
the IT industry. A stalwart in the areas of people management, operations
management and corporate strategy, Mr. Baksi is the President, Corporate Affairs
Tech Mahindra. His track record includes being President - Global Operations,
Customer Services and CEO, HCL Technologies (BPO Solutions and Services).
Mr. Baksi is a graduate of Presidency College, Kolkata and holds a Master's Degree
in Personnel Management from XLRI, Jamshedpur. 
 
 
 
 
 Mr. R S Thakur ( Chairman of TAL Manufacturing Solutions Ltd ) 
 
 
 
 R S Thakur started his career in Union Carbide India Limited as Management
Accountant but quickly moved to TATA Motors Limited (Then TELCO) as Officer
Trainee (Finance) in the plant at Jamshedpur where he spent 28 years heading the
Finance function and then heading the Manufacturing Engineering function before
moving to the Corporate office in Mumbai to head the Finance function
concentrating on Financial restructuring, M & A, Treasury and Business Planning.
Then he moved to Tata Autocomp Systems Limited as MD and CEO where he
turned around the company decisively. He retired from this position at the age of 65
in 2013. His first love is teaching and he has taught MBA classes in various
institutes for thirty years
 
 
 
 
 
 Dr Aquil Busrai ( CEO of Aquil Busrai consulting ) 
 
 
 
 Aquil Busrai has 45 plus years of industry experience in various HR roles. He
was Executive Director-HR at Motorola, Director-Human Resources at Shell
Malaysia, Managing Director of Shell People Services Asia Sdn Bhd, and
Executive Director-Human Resources at IBM India Ltd. He is currently CEO of
Aquil Busrai Consulting. A Gold Medallist from XLRI, Dr. Busrai is on the
National Council of CII, ASSOCHAM, SHRM, and on the board of several
education institutes and Corporate. He is a visiting faculty at Berkeley EMP,
UCLA PGPX, and IIMs in India. He is a fellow of All India Management
Association and Past National President of HRD Network. 
 
 
 
 
 Mr. Ravi Vij ( Entrepreneur And Angel Investor ) 
 
 
 
 Ravi Vij has 22 years of experience in Indian and Multinational Companies across
Automotive and Technology Sector. He is a Fulbright Nehru Research Fellowship
Scholar from Carnegie Mellon University. He has completed his Bachelor of
Mechanical Engineering from Nagpur University and Advanced Accelerated
Management Program from Harvard Business Publishing. Mr. Vij is currently
engaged in incubation of a new business venture in healthcare manufacturing and
services area as an entrepreneur. In his last corporate role, he was working with
Tech Mahindra for 7.3 years and globally lead the P&L for Business
Transformation Services functions which included Automation, Analytics &
Consulting (AAC) portfolios.

title: Training and Placement
content:
Amit Kumar Agrawal 
 
 Incharge- training and career Cell 
 
 E-mail: amitag@iiitnr.edu.in 
 
 
 
 
 
 Mr. Shubham Chandrakar 
 
 Manager - Training, Placement & Entrepreneurship 
 
 Email Id:  shubham@iiitnr.edu.in 
 
 Phone Number:  8085919999 
 
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
 What do we do? 
 
 The Training & Placement Office at DSPM IIIT-Naya Raipur is concerned with every activity that enables students to learn and grow outside the Institute campus. On one hand, it collaborates with leading organizations and institutes in setting up internship and training program for the students while on other it enables the placement of students passing out from the Institute. The office liaises with prospective recruiters such as industrial establishments and corporate houses which conduct campus interviews and select students from both disciplines. It provides infra-structural facilities to conduct group discussions, tests, and interviews and other logistics required for the same. 
 
 The Training and Placement Cell of DSPM IIIT-Naya Raipur strives to get every student trained in the Institute placed in an organization of his or her liking and pursue their passion. To achieve this goal, this office focusses not only on academic skills but also on physical, behavioral and societal skills required to be a team player in the organization. For four years, we nurture our students to develop their all round abilities so as when they land up in the competitive world, they do not lag behind. 
 
   
 
 Why IIIT 
 
 Admission into the B. Tech programs of DSPM-IIIT Naya Raipur takes place through the Joint Entrance Examination (Main). Salient Features of Academic Curriculum:- 
 
 • Basic Motto: Skill First, Theory later, Project integrated 
 
 •Run with Fractal Academic concept, similar to IIT Hyderabad. 
 
 • Common syllabus for both CSE, ECE upto 4 semesters 
 
 • Project in every semester from 5th Semester. 
 
 • Eighth (8) semester dedicated for project work only. 
 
 • Students are allowed to continue the same project from 5th to 8th Semester to increase the depth or they can take up different projects in different domains to increase their breath. 
 
   
 
 Academic Training 
 
   
 
 Skill first 
 
 Click here for some interesting student projects 
 
 DSPM IIIT-Naya Raipur follows an innovative academic structure pioneered and first implemented by IIT-Hyderabad of fractal academics. This is considered as the first step towards experiential learning. At the center of Fractal Academics is the concept of T-concept of education – Breadth first and Depth next. A very similar concept is also being followed by IIT-Bhilai. 
 
 • Under this ‘T’ concept, course content is divided into two parts which require separate training. One part deals with the breadth of the topics and the other deals with depth of the concepts 
 
 • There can be more segments under each part dealing with either breadth or depth. Each segment is considered a fraction, thus it is said to be fractalized (divided) into various segments with 1 or 2 credits. 
 
 • Breadth courses are the basic building block for the depth courses. 
 
 • The fundamental idea is to atomize credits so as to enable the students to get a breadth of knowledge in more number of courses, however, deeper knowledge in the courses of their choice. 
 
 • Breadth courses are taught for first two years followed by depth courses for the remaining two years. 
 
 • Breadth courses are assigned 1 or 2 credits only. A number of such courses are offered to help build a broad perspective. 
 
   
 
 Theory Next 
 
 Depth courses are built on the foundation set by the breadth courses and are of 2 credits or more. Normally these are offered as elective courses. A student interested in pursuing a particular knowledge domain may opt for these courses. 
 
 Application Later 
 
 •Students are then required to work on mini/major projects to implement or understand the proof of concepts learned in depth courses.
